name: Attributes
description: The inborn, most raw aptitudes and potential a character possesses

categories.name: Physical
categories.description: Measure of physical ability. Used to resolve all actions involving fighting, lifting things, running, etc.

traits.name: Strength
traits.description: Indicates how much weight the character can carry, shove or lift. Strength figures into jumping and leaping, and it also measures the raw damage you can deliver in a fight.

traits.name: Dexterity
traits.description: Represents a number of physical aptitudes, including agility, speed and manual deftness. Characters with a high Dexterity have good hand-eye coordination. Generally, they're graceful and light on their feet to boot. Dexterity influences accuracy in combat, as well as the ability to do precision work with one's hands.

traits.name: Stamina
traits.description: Indicates the physical ability to withstand great strain over a long period of time (such as running for hours on end or going without food for days), but it's also a matter of inner resistance (such as holding up in the face of torture or shrugging off dire wounds to finish a mission)

categories.name: Social
categories.description: Measure of charm, attractiveness, and presence. Used to resolve all tasks concerned with trying to coerce, seduce, convince or con someone into doing something.

traits.name: Charisma
traits.description: Represents the character's ability to charm, influence and persuade others. Charisma is also a measure of the character's ability to inspire others, as well as their ability to resist the effects of others' charms.

traits.name: Manipulation
traits.description: Represents the character's ability to control and influence others. Manipulation is a measure of the character's ability to coerce, persuade or deceive others.

traits.name: Appearance
traits.description: Represents the character's physical appearance. Appearance is a measure of the character's ability to attract and influence others.

traits.name: Composure
traits.description: How well the character can maintain and command their own emotions. As well as how easily they can calm nerves and the anxieties of others. It also represents their ability to stay calm-headed in heated situations such as verbal fights and even firefights..

categories.name: Mental
categories.description: Measure of brains, reasoning ability, wits and cunning. Used to resolve all tasks concerned with trying to notice something, puzzle out a clue or think quickly.

traits.name: Intelligence
traits.description: The ability to gather knowledge through research or reason as well as apply logic to an ongoing problem. They have an easier time recalling information they have gathered before and can analyze information from the world around them or the books they've read.

traits.name: Wits
traits.description: The character's ability to think quickly on their feet and make decisions based on the information around them or from prior knowledge. Wits allow the character to pick up sounds and smells around them. It is also representative of a character's street smarts and know what to say in the heat of the moment.

traits.name: Perception
traits.description: Represents the character's ability to notice things, puzzle out a clue or think quickly. Perception is a measure of the character's ability to see through lies, spot hidden dangers or think quickly on their feet.

traits.name: Resolve
traits.description: The raw determination of a character and how strong their focus truly is. Allowing the character to create a mental block from any distractions, showcasing their mental fortitude and concentration

name: Abilities
description: Any skills or aptitudes that your character possesses that make them better at certain activities, whether learned skills or inborn affinities.

categories.name: Physical
categories.description: Physical abilities your character can work out on their own or develop intuitively.

traits.name: Athletics
traits.description: Running, jumping, and climbing are all actions within Athletics. Dodging a well-thrown punch in the heat of combat and throwing an item or weapon is also Athletics.

traits.name: Brawl
traits.description: Combat focused on using the body as a weapon rather than an item.

traits.name: Craft
traits.description: Creating, building, or shaping items such as artwork but also making a makeshift catapult.

traits.name: Dodge
traits.description: The character's ability to avoid attacks or projectiles by moving out of the way or ducking.

traits.name: Drive
traits.description: Driving vehicles specifically under duress and adverse or difficult conditions. Also used for working on cars or general mechanic skills.

traits.name: Firearms
traits.description: Using ranged weaponry such as bows and guns as well as gunsmithing and repair of said items.

traits.name: Larceny
traits.description: Lockpicking a locked door or breaking in other ways, pickpocketing and sleight of hand.

traits.name: Melee
traits.description: Combat focused around using handheld weaponry such as a lead pipe or a baseball bat.

traits.name: Stealth
traits.description: Blending into surroundings to either not be seen or not be recognized, allows characters to move around without alerting anyone of their presence either by sight or sound.

traits.name: Survival
traits.description: Using nature to find shelter, tracking animals or people, and general survival in the wilderness.

categories.name: Social
categories.description: Social abilities your character can use to interact with others.

traits.name: Animal Ken
traits.description: Understanding and reading animal behavior to handle them and work with them.

traits.name: Etiquette
traits.description: The ability to interact in social settings or blend into different groups.

traits.name: Insight
traits.description: Reading body language and other cues to discern emotions, thoughts, or motives.

traits.name: Intimidation
traits.description: Using menacing presence or direct force to ensure compliance, submission, or fear from others. As well as using threats to persuade them to back down.

traits.name: Leadership
traits.description: Motivating, inspiring, or commanding the character's followers.

traits.name: Performance
traits.description: Showcasing the character's art in front of a crowd regardless of size. Singing, acting, and oration are all examples of this.

traits.name: Persuasion
traits.description: Convincing or swaying others to do the character's will or at least agree with their stance.

traits.name: Streetwise
traits.description: Knowledge of the streets in criminal and urban societies, being able to locate a gang or dealer, knowing which shortcut will be the fastest.

traits.name: Subterfuge
traits.description: Deceiving others to complete the character's desires or agree with them or manipulating the truth to further their goals.

categories.name: Mental
categories.description: Mental acuity and learned skills that require focus and concentration.

traits.name: Academics
traits.description: Topics surrounding the humanities and liberal arts.

traits.name: Alertness
traits.description: The character's ability to notice things, puzzle out a clue or think quickly.

traits.name: Awareness
traits.description: Vigilant to their surroundings and the ability to understand/react to threats.

traits.name: Bureaucracy
traits.description: Understanding and navigating the rules and regulations of a government or organization.

traits.name: Finance
traits.description: Knowing how to move and make money legally or not, being able to handle finances and their taxes.

traits.name: Investigation
traits.description: Being able to find and follow clues, decipher their meaning and solve a mystery.

traits.name: Medicine
traits.description: Handling mortal injuries and diagnosing/treating diseases.

traits.name: Occult
traits.description: Mystical knowledge of the supernatural and rituals.

traits.name: Politics
traits.description: Knowing the inner workings of the government, mortal or not.

traits.name: Science
traits.description: Scientific knowledge and theory of the real world.

traits.name: Technology
traits.description: Internet and computer comprehension, hacking and breaking into secure information through the use of technology.

categories.name: Disciplines
categories.description: The supernatural powers a vampire possesses.

traits.name: Animalism
traits.description: Utilized by those who desire a close and supernatural bond with the animal world as well as a vampire's own Beast.

traits.name: Auspex
traits.description: Enables users to hone their senses both physical and psychic in order to bolster their awareness, perceptions, or even see visions of the future.

traits.name: Blood Sorcery
traits.description: A type of blood magic that allows the practitioners to manipulate the blood, mortal or vampiric.

traits.name: Celerity
traits.description: Powers up the movement of the user, enabling them to have unnatural quickness in their movement and supernatural reflexes.

traits.name: Chimerstry
traits.description: The art of conjuration that converts the vampire's will into phantoms that confound the senses and technology alike

traits.name: Dominate
traits.description: Allows the user to use mind control through eye contact and spoken word as well as manipulate memories of their victims.

traits.name: Fortitude
traits.description: Strengthens the user's physical and mental resistance.

traits.name: Necromancy
traits.description: Learn to command the souls of the undead to your bidding.

traits.name: Obfuscate
traits.description: The art of not being seen even in crowds either through being wholly unseen or by blending in.

traits.name: Obeah
traits.description: The Salubri bloodline's Discipline known to its Healer Class. Salubri Healers are valorous combat healers, and with the power of Obeah, they heal the gravest of injuries, so that the righteous may fight on.

traits.name: Oblivion
traits.description: Has two branches: one allows the user to manipulate shadows at will and the other enables necromancy or usage of spirits, though both tap into the Abyss.

traits.name: Quietus
traits.description: Based on elements of blood alteration, vitae control, and pestilence, Quietus focuses on weakening and injuring a target so that she may be more easily destroyed.

traits.name: Potence
traits.description: Strengthens the user's physical prowess.

traits.name: Presence
traits.description: Enables the user to use subtle manipulation, control, and swaying of emotions to guide others towards a goal.

traits.name: Protean
traits.description: Grants the ability to change one's shape, grow claws, meld into the earth, or become fog.

traits.name: Serpentis
traits.description: Provides shapechanging abilities, though it also encompasses corruption and other characteristics associated with their God and his victories over Apep

traits.name: Thaumaturgy
traits.description: The art of refining and altering one's blood to improve one's physical and mental abilities as well as granting the ability to transform into mist or cloud, among other abilities.

traits.name: Thin Blood Alchemy
traits.description: The art of refining and altering one's blood to improve one's physical and mental abilities as well as granting the ability to transform into mist or cloud, among other abilities.

traits.name: Vicissitude
traits.description: The creation of mixtures of blood, emotions, and other varied ingredients to activate unique effects or even copy other Disciplines.

subcategories.name: Arsenal
subcategories.description: Either borrowed from an old contact or put together in a garage, the Hunter has access to a wide variety of weapons that are generally not available to the public. This includes not only firearms but also melee weapons; however, this does not include explosives such as bombs, mines, or other munitions. These weapons are still mundane and are only available for a short period of time, either having to be returned to the contact, undergo severe maintenance, or break down due to their nature of being jerry-rigged.
subcategories.pool: `Intelligence` + `Craft` for the Hunter who keeps and maintains their own arsenal. `Manipulation` + `Streetwise` for the Hunter who relies on contacts.
subcategories.system: The Hunter is able to provide a single personal firearm or melee weapon upon winning a test at Difficulty 4. This test is made at the start of a scene, and the Hunter must have plausible access to their supplies or contacts. It can only be attempted once per scene, and the weapon can only be used for that scene, but later tests can provide the same or a similar weapon.

traits.name: Team Requisition
traits.description: Up to the margin of the win, the hunter can provide additional copies of the same weapon.

traits.name: Exotics
traits.description: The Hunter is able to procure rare or one-of-a-kind weapons or ammunition. The Difficulty of the test can increase in the case of something extremely rare or unique, as determined by the Storyteller.

traits.name: Backup Piece
traits.description: As long as they can hide the piece on their person, once per scene, they can declare a weapon. This occurs even if the Hunter fails the test to activate this Edge. The weapon has no Perks and can be stolen or otherwise detected by ordinary means. If it is lost, a backup cannot be acquired until the next scene.

traits.name: Special Features
traits.description: The weapon obtained comes with a number of special features, up to the margin of the win. The features can range from thermal signs to disguised cases, the Storyteller makes the final decision of what qualifies as a special feature.

traits.name: Untraceable
traits.description: The weapons are completely untraceable and will never lead authorities or quarry to the Hunters.

subcategories.name: Fleet
subcategories.description: Either borrowed from a reliable contact or put together in a garage, the Hunter has access to a wide variety of personal or commercial vehicles on short notice. This does not include military vehicles such as tanks or attack aircraft.
subcategories.pool: `Intelligence` + `Technology` for the Hunter who keeps and maintains their own fleet. `Manipulation` + `Persuasion` for the Hunter who relies on contacts.
subcategories.system: The Hunter is able to provide a single vehicle for either public or commercial use, including boats and aircraft, upon winning a test at Difficulty 4. Outlandish requests should be vetted by the Storyteller with increasing Difficulty as needed. This test is made at the start of a scene, and the Hunter must have plausible access to their garage or contact. It can only be attempted once per scene, and the vehicle can only be used for that scene, but later tests can provide the same or a similar vehicle. This Edge does not grant the ability to pilot the vehicles provided.

traits.name: Armor
traits.description: Vehicles are armored to withstand small firearms. Flying vehicles benefit less from the armor but do provide additional protection to passengers, adding two dice to their defense pools against ranged weaponry.

traits.name: Surveillance
traits.description: Vehicles come with a wide variety of concealed surveillance tools. This equipment gives bonus dice equal to the margin on the Edge test on stakeouts from the vehicle, such as Awareness or Technology tests. However, this bonus cannot exceed three dice.

traits.name: Hidden Cache
traits.description: The vehicle has a hidden compartment. The difficulty is increased by 2 to locate the compartment based on the Difficulty of the area they are searching. Should the cache not be discovered, they can use all their Arseal and Ordinance Edges even if the rest of the gear is taken. The limit of items stored is at storyteller discretion.

traits.name: Performance
traits.description: Vehicles have superior driving and handling. This gives a bonus equal to the margin on the Edge test to win against pursuit-related Driving tests, however, this bonus cannot be more than 3 dice.

traits.name: Untraceable
traits.description: The vehicles are completely untraceable and will never lead authorities or quarry to the Hunters.

traits.name: Wagon Train
traits.description: If the Hunter loses a vehicle they can make a second test of their Edge during the scene to obtain a new one. This vehicle either lacks one Perk, or increases the tests Difficulty while using it by 1.

subcategories.name: Ordinance
subcategories.description: Either obtained through one or many connections or put together by the Hunter themselves, the Hunter has access to various types of timed or remotely detonated munitions. Examples of these are anti-vehicle mines, C4 explosives, or demolition charges.
subcategories.pool: `Composure` + `Science` for the Hunter who builds their own ordnance. `Composure` + `Streetwise` for the Hunter who relies on contacts.
subcategories.system: The Hunter is able to provide a single mine or explosive upon winning a test at Difficulty 4. This test is made at the start of a scene and the Hunter must have plausible access to their supplies or contacts. It can only be attempted once per scene, and the explosive can only be used for that scene.

traits.name: Multiple Payloads
traits.description: Up to the margin of the win, the hunter can provide additional copies of the same explosive.

traits.name: Exotics
traits.description: Custom or otherwise rare substances can be obtained. The Storyteller should alter the Difficulty of the test in cases of exceedingly rare items. These items are not inherently supernatural but can exploit supernatural vulnerabilities.

traits.name: Non-lethal Munitions
traits.description: The Hunter is able to obtain non-lethal munitions such as flash grenades or tear gas. The Storyteller should determine the effects of these substances on others, a good rule of thumb is a dice penalty equal to the margin of the Edge test. This penalty cannot exceed three dice.

traits.name: Disguised Delivery
traits.description: Items obtained are disguised as mundane items. The Difficulty to detect them is increased by the margin on the Edge test, but this penalty cannot exceed more than three dice.

subcategories.name: Library
subcategories.description: The Hunter has access to a wealth of information on a wide variety of topics and creatures through either a library filled with centuries-old texts or an encrypted database with all the latest research on the supernatural.
subcategories.system: The Hunter must spend about a day researching their quarry before making an Edge test. The Storyteller decides what type of clue they receive upon a win and how vague it is, with the clarity and amount of information increasing based on the margin of the win. This can only be attempted once per scene, and once the Hunter succeeds, it cannot be used again until the next session.

traits.name: Where They Hide
traits.description: In addition to the clues obtained, any attempts to locate where the creature's lair is receive a bonus. This bonus is equal to the margin of the Edge test; it expires after use and cannot be greater than three dice.

traits.name: How to Halt Them
traits.description: In addition to the clues obtained, any attempts to ward or otherwise protect an area or person from the prey receive a bonus. This bonus is equal to the margin of the Edge test; it expires after use and cannot be greater than three dice. This does not apply to any direct attacks.

traits.name: Binge
traits.description: Research time is cut in half.

traits.name: Group Study
traits.description: The Hunter may add 1 bonus die per cell member participating in the research, even if they do not have the Library Edge.

traits.name: How to Silence Them
traits.description: Gain bonus equal to margin to any attempt to damage the target in social combat. Bonus expires after use and cannot exceed 3 dice.

traits.name: Where they Go
traits.description: In addition to the clue learned, the information grants an additional bonus equal to the margin on the win to attempts to identify the prey's preferred targets, hunting grounds, or who they may attack next. This bonus expires after use and cannot exceed three dice.

traits.name: Who They Are
traits.description: In addition to the clues obtained, any attempts to identify the prey receive a bonus. This bonus is equal to the margin of the Edge test; it expires after use and cannot be greater than three dice.

traits.name: How to Harm Them
traits.description: In addition to the clues obtained, any attempts to harm the prey by exploiting their supernatural weaknesses receive a bonus. This bonus is equal to the margin of the Edge test; it expires after use and cannot be greater than three dice.

traits.name: Friendly Librarian
traits.description: If the character can wait one or two days before the Edge roll, they gain one automatic success.

traits.name: Permanent Fixture
traits.description: The campus library can be used as a safe house once per semester.

traits.name: Pattern Analysis
traits.description: Able to narrow down specific monster behavior easily, alongside finding people alive to talk to who are familiar with the phenomenon.

subcategories.name: Experimental Medicine
subcategories.description: Whether it's drugs or medicine, the Hunter has an indefinite waiver to a medical team to perform whatever experiments on them in exchange for swift, free medical care.
subcategories.system: Whenever the Hunter has one or more Aggravated damage, they may undergo this experimental procedure, Difficulty equal to half the subject's current amount of Aggravated damage (rounded up). Winning heals completely, failing inflicts the appropriate Impaired status for the story's duration and raises Danger by 1.

traits.name: Improved Resilience
traits.description: Until the end of the next story, the Hunter counts as having a Armor Value of 1 when unarmored, stacking with worn armor.

traits.name: Phoenix Protocol
traits.description: For the duration of the next story, the Hunter heals Health twice as quickly, and Aggravated damage downgraded to Superficial. However, they can't use Drive for Desperation dice. When effect ends, they gain Aggravated Willpower damage equal to the times this Perk was used.

traits.name: Monstrous Enhancement
traits.description: One of the Hunter's Attributes is increased by 2 dots (To max of 5), but develop a weakness to a common, mundane material (e.g. Silver, Seawater), taking Aggravated damage from it until the story's end.

traits.name: Unstable Steroids
traits.description: One of the Hunter's Attributes is increased by 1 until the end of the next story. Should they achieve Despair on any tests before that, they also suffer 1 Aggravated damage.

subcategories.name: Improvised Gear
subcategories.description: With the objects and materials nearby, the Hunter is able to create any number of short-lived but useful tools to aid the cell.
subcategories.pool: Intelligence + Craft/Technology/Science depending on the tool or substance being made.
subcategories.system: The Hunter is able to provide short-lived but useful equipment from ordinary items upon winning a test at Difficulty 4. This item can add to the use of a single Skill by adding a two-dice advantage at the Storyteller's discretion. It can also make certain actions that would have been impossible possible for the Hunters. The amount of time needed to create this item is determined by the Storyteller and may range from a few minutes to several hours. Creating these under duress, such as a firefight, is not possible. These items only last through one scene, and unless they have the associated perks, Hunters can only make one per scene.

traits.name: Frugal
traits.description: With their trinkets and tools on their person in a bag or another container, the Hunter can apply this Edge anywhere.

traits.name: Specialization
traits.description: Specializing in a specific Skill, the Hunter now can produce items that have a 3-dice bonus instead of 2. This perk can be taken multiple times, but only once per Skill.

traits.name: Made to Last
traits.description: Generally, improvised weapons last for a single scene. This Perk allows it to last for additional scenes equal to success margin. It has a minimum of 1. This may continue into the next session; they are not stronger nor sturdier than other items made with this Edge. At ST discretion, they can break before the additional scenes run out.

traits.name: Mass Production
traits.description: The Hunter can produce additional items equal to the margin of the test. These items are, however, all identical and grant the bonus to the same Skill. All other restrictions and Perks apply.

traits.name: Speed Crafting
traits.description: This Edge can now be used under duress, such as in firefights, and the Hunter crafts the tool in three turns minus the margin of the test. There is a minimum of one turn to create the item.

subcategories.name: Global Access
subcategories.description: The Hunter is able to accomplish feats of digital larceny well beyond what a normal hacker is able to.
subcategories.system: To use this Edge, the Hunter must spend the equivalent of a scene (the length of time being determined by the Storyteller). The Hunter is then able to obtain data on any subject or person in any database, ranging from surveillance to buried records, upon winning a test at Difficulty 4. Without the associated Perks, this data may not be manipulated, and in the case of non-networked systems, the Hunter must have physical access to the local network.

traits.name: Watching Big Brother
traits.description: The Hunter can manipulate digital surveillance footage, editing people in and out of the records.

traits.name: Money Trap
traits.description: The Hunter can manipulate financial data and move money with little effort. This can be used to deprive prey or enemies of financial assets or guide authoritarian eyes toward something suspicious. This lowers the level of Resources of a target, when used offensively, by one per margin of the test. This reduction lasts for one month. The Storyteller should decide how this affects the Hunters when they use it for personal gain, with at least an increase in Danger as they line their own pockets.

traits.name: Digital Cannon Fodder
traits.description: Reduce the success of any attempt to digitally surveil, trace or locate the Hunter by 2.

traits.name: Spoof
traits.description: Pin an intrusion on a fake person or another real person. This may not fool everyone. The difficulty is determined by the ST and ranges 3-5 depending on how untraceable the spoof is.

traits.name: All-Access Pass
traits.description: The Hunter can bypass electronic locks, tamper with, or disable security countermeasures such as alarm systems. The Difficulty depends on what the system is, with it usually being between 3-5 as determined by the Storyteller.

traits.name: The Letter of the Law
traits.description: The Hunter can manipulate criminal records. Making their enemies wanted criminals or erasing another's mistakes. The Difficulty ranges from 3 (local offenses) to 5 (global scale) when attempting to wipe or place them on someone else.

traits.name: Intranet Insertion
traits.description: The Hunter gains access to non-networked or air gapped systems without a need to be physically present. They may use the Edge and any Perks as normal. Unless they cover their tracks, the remote intrusion of a network is easily noticed. At ST discretion, using this Perk may automatically increase Danger.

subcategories.name: Drone Jockey
subcategories.description: The Hunter has a drone that surpasses those that are commercially available. They are also able to control it expertly and repair it on their own. The type of drone varies, either as a tool for surveillance or for combat.
subcategories.pool: `Wits` + `Technology` for flying the drone. `Intelligence` + `Craft` to either repair or rebuild it.
subcategories.system:
When obtained, the drone is equipped to perform two Skills. For example, a walking drone with armor might be able to perform `Athletics` and `Brawl`; a surveillance drone might perform `Stealth` and `Awareness`. The type of drone that a Hunter uses is determined when the Edge is obtained, and more variants may be obtained through the Perks. When a controller tests the Skill of the drone, either through the hand-held device or hardwired computer, they use their `Wits` + `Technology` instead of the usual pool. If the drone must make a Skill test for a Skill they do not possess, they use a one-die pool.
The drone has an unlimited range, and can stay active for one day plus additional days equal to the margin of a test made of `Intelligence` + `Science` at Difficulty 2. Drones have five health levels, with ground drones treating damage as Superficial and flying drones treating damage as Aggravated. By spending the equivalent of a scene and making a test of `Intelligence` + `Craft` at Difficulty 3, the Hunter may repair the damage of a drone by the number of health equal to the margin of the test. This can also be done on destroyed drones, but they must be fully restored before they can be used again.

traits.name: Autonomous
traits.description: The Hunter is able to run drones with simple patterns on their own. Complicated decision trees require a `Resolve` + `Technology` test as an appropriate difficulty. Instead of using the Hunter's `Wits` + `Technology`, any Skills used in this mode use a flat pool of five dice.

traits.name: Specialist Skill
traits.description: This drone can obtain an additional Skill, and if the Storyteller agrees, this can also include the drone carrying items or manipulating the environment. Some examples are `Larceny` with a set of electronic picks or `Science` with an onboard mobile lab. This Perk only applies to one drone but can be taken multiple times for additional drones.

traits.name: Payload
traits.description: The drone can carry cargo many times bigger than what it would seem to be able to carry. While carrying cargo, the drone moves slower and grants two-dice to those attempting to detect it. This drone will not subdue a resisting cargo, and the size of the drone should be agreed upon by the Storyteller and player before it's used.

traits.name: Variants
traits.description: This Perk can be taken multiple times and allows an additional drone variant (complete with two Skills unrelated to previous drones). Only one drone can be active at a time, unless the Autonomous Perk is used to run them.

traits.name: Armaments
traits.description: The drone is equipped with the equivalent of a submachine gun or taser, which uses a flat five-dice pool. The Specialist Skill Perk allows the controller to use their `Wits` + `Technology` when directly controlling the drone.

traits.name: Electronic Shield
traits.description: Protection against signal interference or hacking. Unless a skill or ability states otherwise, the drone cannot be hacked or tampered with. Should a skill give the ability to break the defenses, the Difficulty is 2 plus the margin of the operator's `Intelligence` + `Technology` test at Difficulty 2.

subcategories.name: Beast Whisperer
subcategories.description: Some species of animals may be understood by and loyal to the Hunter. Thus, they are dependable companions and occasionally even friends.
subcategories.pool: `Charisma` + `Animal Ken` for commands, `Composure` + `Animal Ken` for training.
subcategories.system: When taking this Edge this Hunter must choose one type of animal and which key action pool it has. This animal is considered completely loyal and will accompany a Hunter into any situation as long as it is desired.

traits.name: Incorruptible
traits.description: The animal is immune to supernatural powers that would sway it away from its master.

traits.name: Complex Commands
traits.description: Animals can now understand more complex commands. `Charisma` + `Animal Ken` is used to give these commands with a Difficult dependent on their complexity, while `Intelligence` + `Animal Ken` is used to understand the animal.

traits.name: Supernatural Scent
traits.description: Once trained over the course of a story, the animal can track a specific supernatural type by its senses. The supernatural must have a distinct stimulus, and the scent cannot be older than 12 hours. It can be learned multiple times, but only for different creature types.

traits.name: Menagerie
traits.description: Choose another animal type and add it to the available pool of animals.

traits.name: Incognito
traits.description: The animal can now stay out of sight, hiding either just out of observed range or blending into environments. The Storyteller should determine the plausibility of this in certain situations, depending on the animal.

subcategories.name: Turncoat
subcategories.description: The Hunter is a double agent, working with their cell to get on the inside with the Quarry. The Quarry fully believing in their loyalty completely.
subcategories.system: The Hunter makes an Edge test vs Difficulty equaling half the target's `Intelligence` + `Wits` or standard Difficulty. Target now believes the Hunter is 100% loyal to them.

traits.name: Deathbed Confession
traits.description: Able to use Turncoat edge during combat, taking a full action.

traits.name: Stick to the Plan
traits.description: Cell is always firmly in sync with the Hunter, able to understand the Turncoat Hunter's intent without communication needed.

traits.name: Poker Face
traits.description: 2-dice bonus to Turncoat Edges when questioned.

traits.name: We Come as a Team
traits.description: For each point of margin, the Hunter can bring a cellmate along. The Hunter can make Turncoat Edge test to vouch for them.

subcategories.name: Sense the Unnatural
subcategories.description: While holding an object of focus, the Hunter is able to detect the nearby presence of supernatural creatures. However, this ability does not determine who or what the creature is.
subcategories.pool: `Wits` + `Occult` or `Science`, depending on the Endowment's nature.
subcategories.system: Upon winning an Edge test with a Difficulty of 3-5 (ranging on the type of creature and its power), the Hunter will be able to detect if there is a presence. The Hunter must hold their object of focus, with its use being intentional and obvious. In order to use this power more than once per scene, it requires spending one point of Willpower for each additional attempt.

traits.name: Creature Specialization
traits.description: Gain a two-dice bonus against a specific type of creature when attempting to detect it. This can be learned multiple times, but only once for each creature type.

traits.name: Precision
traits.description: The Hunter is now able to determine who the supernatural creature in the room is, but they are unable to determine the type unless they have a specialization for it.

traits.name: Horrid Detail
traits.description: Cut through any supernatural disguises to sense the Quarry's real or non-physical form.

traits.name: Range
traits.description: The range of the ability is extended to roughly the size of a city block, but it does not give precision beyond if it's in the same room or farther away.

traits.name: Handsfree
traits.description: The Hunter no longer needs to use an object of focus for this Edge.

traits.name: Network
traits.description: With a `Wits` + `Insight` test, they can determine if anyone is under the influence or had recent contact with the supernatural. If the influencer is nearby, they can also see the connection between them. This does not inform them about the type of influence or the relationship type between them.

subcategories.name: Repel the Unnatural
subcategories.description: By brandishing or otherwise using an object of focus, the Hunter is able to repel supernatural creatures.
subcategories.pool: `Resolve` + `Occult` or `Science` depending on the Endowment's nature.
subcategories.system: Upon winning an Edge test with a Difficulty set by the Storyteller (Default is half the creature's `Composure` + `Resolve` or just the standard Difficulty of the creature), the creature can be held back by the Hunter as long as they remain stationary with their object of focus. When repelled, creatures cannot move closer or engage them in brawl, but they are able to defend themselves and use any of their other powers. Should the Hunter move, they must make the Edge test for each turn spent moving with the same pool and Difficulty as before. If they lose, the repelling stops and cannot be used on the same creature again for the duration of the scene.

traits.name: Ward
traits.description: The area of protection can be extended to include roughly two meters around the Hunter, with an additional meter per margin of success.

traits.name: Creature Specialization
traits.description: Gain a two-dice bonus against a specific type of creature when attempting to repel it. This can be learned multiple times, but only once for each creature type.

traits.name: Damage
traits.description: The Hunter can use the object of their focus as a melee weapon with +0 damage and inflict Aggravated damage to the creature under the repel effect. If used in this manner, the Hunter must immediately retake the Edge test, or else its protective power fails.

traits.name: Handsfree
traits.description: The Hunter no longer needs to use an object of focus for this Edge.

subcategories.name: Thwart the Unnatural
subcategories.description: Using an object of focus, the Hunter is highly resistant to any supernatural abilities that affect the mind.
subcategories.pool: `Composure` + `Occult` or `Science`, depending on the nature of the Endowment.
subcategories.system: While holding their object of focus, a Hunter is immune to supernatural abilities that would otherwise require a resistance test. For abilities that would automatically affect them, the Hunter may instead resist with an Edge test. This only applies to attempts that target the Hunter specifically and not to purely physical attacks or augmentations. For example, this would protect against mind control, but not against a creature attempting to rip them into pieces with their claws.

traits.name: Creature Specialization
traits.description: Gain a two-dice bonus against a specific type of creature when attempting to resist it. This can be learned multiple times, but only once for each creature type

traits.name: Recognition
traits.description: If they successfully resist an ability, the Hunter is made aware of the attempt and what the power would have done. This, however, does not give them the exact rules but instead gives hints.

traits.name: Redirection
traits.description: When they successfully resist an ability, they may take one point of Superficial Willpower damage and redirect the ability to impact the user instead. If they do not have the Recognition Perk, the ST does not have to reveal the ability and may inflict a lesser version onto the user.

traits.name: Ward
traits.description: The area of protection can be extended to include roughly two meters around the Hunter, with an additional meter per margin of success. Anyone in the area receives the benefit of this Edge, with the Hunter who possesses it making all the resistance tests and spending the Willpower.

traits.name: Handsfree
traits.description: The Hunter no longer needs to use an object of focus for this Edge.

subcategories.name: Artifact
subcategories.description: The Hunter has obtained a rare tool, whatever it is, that provides more than a similar object would.
subcategories.pool: `Intelligence` + `Occult` or `Science`, depending on the Artifact's nature.
subcategories.system: The relic provides the Hunter with a one-dice bonus to a single Skill associated with it, however, this bonus does not apply to Edge tests for the Artifact. This can be used as an object of focus if it meets the requirements. Unlike other objects of focus, though, if it is lost, it requires a long ordeal to regain it.

traits.name: Empower
traits.description: Once per scene, the Hunter can make an Edge test at Difficulty 4 to increase the bonus dice to three. If this test fails, they suffer superficial Willpower damage equal to the amount they lost.

traits.name: Detection
traits.description: The Artifact acts similar to Sense the Unnatural Edge and uses the same base system rules.

traits.name: Feature Unlocked
traits.description: Once per story, when `Danger` is at 5, the Artifact allows to spend a Willpower to reroll as many non-Desperation dice as the Hunter likes. Successes are treated as critical wins.

traits.name: Attraction
traits.description: Many people seek out this item, and so it can be used as bait and provides a two-dice bonus to any ambush attempt. Be warned: once it's revealed, those who know will continue to look for it as long as they know its location.

traits.name: Shield
traits.description: Acting as a supernatural shield for the Hunter, while it's on their person, any physical damage stemming from supernatural sources is halved. (Or halved again, in cases of superficial damage.)

subcategories.name: Cleanse the Unnatural
subcategories.description: It is the ability to drive out supernatural influence from a victim, it removes the taint of supernatural control from a person and gives them back their freedom.
subcategories.system: Hunter makes an Edge test vs Difficulty dependent on duration of supernatural influence (1 if started on same scene, 2 if past 24 hours, 3 for a week, 4 for year, 5 for anything beyond). Targets take 3 Aggravated damage (Health/Willpower), minus 1 for each point of margin. Upon success, target regains full control for 24 hours.

traits.name: Bedside Manner
traits.description: Any damage dealt by this Edge becomes Superficial and after halving, is rounded down.

traits.name: Trace the Threads
traits.description: The Storyteller will answer 1 question about the supernatural controller's current location for each point of the margin.

traits.name: Inflict Stigmata
traits.description: Inflicting a Stigmata causes 1 additional point of Aggravated Health damage but reduces Difficulty of Edge test by 2. Stigmata are permanent.

traits.name: Psychic Backlash
traits.description: For every 2 point of the margin, the controlling entity receives 1 Aggravated damage. (Hunter chooses either `Health` or `Willpower`)

subcategories.name: Great Destiny
subcategories.description: The Hunter is empowered to fulfill a higher purpose.
subcategories.system: The Hunter chooses a higher purpose or destiny. At the beginning of each session, the Hunter creates a temporary pool of 2 dice, which may be added to any dice pools that further their destiny. This pool resets at the beginning of each session.

traits.name: Divine Protection
traits.description: When the Hunter takes Health damage in service of their destiny, they can reduce the damage by 2 (minimum of 0).

traits.name: Sacred Insight
traits.description: Once per story, the Hunter can get a supernatural voice/vision providing a clue to help fulfill their destiny.

traits.name: Heavenly Resolve
traits.description: When the Hunter takes damage in Social Combat defending or proselytizing their destiny, can restore 1 Aggravated Willpower damage.

traits.name: Influence Fate
traits.description: Once per session, the Hunter may influence a target to take 1 action that would aid in the Hunter's destiny. `Charisma` + `Occult` vs target's `Resolve` + `Occult`.

subcategories.name: Unnatural Changes
subcategories.description: When holding or using an object of focus, they can use their bodies in a way no standard human could. General objects of focus might be a stash of injectable drugs, a radioactive object, a piece of a Quarry's body that will be eaten, or a type of crystal that opens them to supernatural energies.
subcategories.system: System: When taking this Edge, the character must select one Attribute it will apply to. They may take this Edge more than once with the Breadth Perk to take a different Attribute. This can only enhance one at a time, it cannot be used to stack on the same Attribute. They must test at a Difficulty 4 using the Edge pool. On a success, for the rest of the scene, they add two dice to all rolls using the selected Attribute. Should they fail, the suffer Aggravated Damage of either `Health` or `Willpower` or a mixture, equal to the margin of failure. Entering Despair when this Edge is active ends the effects prematurely.

traits.name: Breadth
traits.description: Select a second Attribute to enhance. They gain 1 bonus die to all pools using the Attribute.

traits.name: Neuropathway Practice
traits.description: The Edge's Difficulty is reduced from 4 to 3.

traits.name: Maximuzed Neuropathways
traits.description: Activation does not use an action.

traits.name: Handsfree
traits.description: They no longer rely on the object of focus to activate.

traits.name: Blissful Ignorance
traits.description: By remaining completely still, the Garou can become invisible to others, including monitoring devices and most spirits. Any attempt to spot the Garou is done at Difficulty 2+ the Gift user's Wisdom. Movement breaks the effect. This is granted by a chameleon-spirit. When used in attempt to ambush it follows the normal ambush rules, allowing the target a chance to spot the attacker, Wits + Awareness against the attacker's Composure + Stealth.

traits.name: Crow's Laughter
traits.description: A target within sight and hearing can be enticed to attack the user. This is granted by a crow-spirit. Upon winning the test, the target must attack the user or spend one point of Willpower to resist. This gift can also be used in homid form.

traits.name: Spider's Song
traits.description: The Garou can eavesdrop on any long-distance verbal conversations. Landline conversations must be listened through a telephone wire or cord, to hear cellphones they only need to see the device. This is granted by a spider-spirit. On the Gift test, a few successes give scattered words, whereas five or more (or critical wins) allow them to hear every word and possibly identify the voices. Encryption increases the Difficulty by 1 or more. This gift can also be used in homid form.

traits.name: Umbral Pocket
traits.description: Send a small object hidden on one's person into the Umbra. The item cannot be found except by those who can see into the Umbra, and can be retrieved by the Gift's user at will.

traits.name: Blur of the Milky Eye
traits.description: The Garou gains their Wisdom bonus to all stealth tests and physical defensive attacks. This is granted by a chameleon-spirit. This Gift ends when they directly interact with a subject, such as attack or conversation.

traits.name: Open Seal
traits.description: Mechanical locks or bars open automatically. Electronic or supernatural devices required a Gift test with a Difficulty set by the Storyteller. This is granted by a raccoon-spirit. The Difficulty can be 2 for regular electronic locks up to 5 for sorcery augmented fingerprint scanners. In some cases, locks may be beyond this Gift's ability to open as determined by the Storyteller.

traits.name: Pulse of the Prey
traits.description: The werewolf can track down an individual. Humans who are not hiding do not require a Gift test to be located. This is granted by a dog-spirit. Supernatural or humans who are actively hiding must be tested against. Time required depends on the distance to the target and the result of the roll, supernatural ways to hide can increase the Difficulty. This gift can also be used in lupus form.

traits.name: Scent of Running Water
traits.description: The Garou can make themselves impossible to track through mundane means and difficulty through supernatural means by removing their physical evidence of passing through. This is granted by a fox-spirit. They also gain their Wisdom as a bonus to avoid supernatural tracking. This Gift is free to activate, but they can spend 1 Willpower to extend the Gift's effects to their pack for one scene. This gift can also be used in lupus form.

traits.name: Luna's Blessing
traits.description: With the moon visible to the Garou, they howl and are no longer weak to silver. This is granted by a Lune. Silver damage is taken, up to the Wisdom level of the user, as Superficial.

traits.name: Thieving Talons of the Magpie
traits.description: The Garou can steal the supernatural powers (Gifts, vampire powers or equivalents) of others for their own use. The ability must be learned by the target and witnessed by the user. This power becomes unavailable to the original owner while in Garou's possession. Granted by magpie spirit. The Gift test is made against the level of the power, such as Rank of a Gift and if another supernatural trait, the Storyteller determines it's appropriate Difficulty. The Garou is able to use it upon the next turn, using Honor as a replacement for any non-Garou traits needed.

gift_attributes.duration: One turn for each Willpower spent

traits.name: The Thousand Forms
traits.description: Transform into any mundane animal, from a bear to a chicken. They are still limited in communication as when in lupus form. Particularly small, large or exotic forms may increase the Difficulty by 1 or more as determined by the Storyteller. This is granted by a Wyld-spirit. Upon a fail, the user can attempt again by making another Rage Check and adding the successes from their previous attempt. The user keeps their Mental Attributes but their other pools depend on the shape taken. This gift can be used in any form.

traits.name: Whelp Body
traits.description: Weaken and shrivel the body of a target, the use of this Gift on another Garou is considered a dire affront. This is granted by a disease-spirit. This can be used on a target within sight and five meters. For each success from the user's margin, the target loses a single dot from one of their Physical Attributes with a minimum of 0. The effects last for the rest of the story, but can become permanent up to the Storyteller's discretion.

traits.name: Ensnare Spirit
traits.description: Weaken a spirit within the same realm, giving them a two-dice penalty to resist the user's will. This can also stop a spirit unless they are able to break free with a resistance roll. This is granted by a weaver-spirit of law. This Gift does not work on hostile spirits and attempts to harm the spirit breaks the Gift.

traits.name: Mother's Touch
traits.description: Heal other creatures with a Gift test, for as many successes rolled. However, this Gift cannot heal spirits or the undead. This is granted by a bear-spirit. If the amount of successes is greater than the subject's Rage, it is possible to heal one Aggravated instead of one Superficial. This gift can also be used in any form.

traits.name: Shadow Sense
traits.description: Determine if supernatural creatures or elements are nearby without pinpointing the exact location or identity. This is granted by a raven-spirit. The Difficulty of the Gift test is 2, but can be modified by powers that allow creatures to hide their supernatural features.

traits.name: Sight from Beyond
traits.description: The Garou receives unclear visions, that can warn for future choices or immediate danger. This can be used either by choice once per scene by spending 1 Willpower and making the Gift test or given freely by the Storyteller. This is granted by a dream-spirit. When given freely, the Storyteller determines if a Gift test is needed, with the amount of successes determining how clear it is and what clues are gained.

traits.name: Entreat
traits.description: Temporarily bond with a spirit to gain some of its abilities. Although the spirit does not need to be willing, it cannot be in active conflict with the Gift user.

traits.name: Summon Implement
traits.description: Instantly summon a talisman or Dedicated item. The summoned item must belong to or be Dedicated to the Gift user.

traits.name: Grasp from Beyond
traits.description: Bring objects in and out of the Umbra. This is granted by a magpie, beaver, or possum spirit. Willpower amount needed depends on the size of the object with a small object being 1 and large objects being 3.

traits.name: Mindspeak
traits.description: Send and receive thoughts to a single subject, which can establish two-way communication. This Gift does not allow the user to read minds. This Gift can also be used in any form.

gift_attributes.duration: One scene or until contact breaks

traits.name: Umbral Tether
traits.description: Create a thread that allows the user to find their way back to the Umbra entry point. This also grants their Wisdom rating as bonus dice to navigate the Spirit Wilds. This Gift can also be used in any form.

traits.name: Bolt Hole
traits.description: Retreat into a personal, room-sized pocket of the Umbra. Additional people up to the margin of the win can be brought inside by holding onto the user while the Gift is used.

traits.name: Command Spirit
traits.description: Allows the Garou to issue commands to a spirit that must be followed through upon. This is granted by a Incarna avatar. This Gift does not work to summon spirits, banish spirits, or inflict harm on themselves.

traits.name: Drain Spirit
traits.description: Suck the essence of a spirit and heal superficial Willpower. This is granted by a leach or lamprey-spirit. Failure of the contest gives one point of Aggravated Willpower damage to the user. This can only affect the same spirit once per scene.

traits.name: Feral Regression
traits.description: Erode a victims Mental Attributes, turning them more animal-like. This is granted by Various Wyld gafflings. The effects last for the rest of the story, they can be become permanent dependent on the Storyteller. Attributes cannot go below 0.

traits.name: Living Ward
traits.description: The Garou can turn themselves into a ward against spirits, preventing them from coming within three meters. This is granted by a legendary wolf-spirit. They cannot perform any other actions during this.

traits.name: Gaia's Candor
traits.description: Determine if someone believes their answer is the truth based on scent. This is granted by a truth-spirit. No test is required for humans unaware of the Garou. Victims can refuse to answer.

traits.name: Porcupine's Reprisal
traits.description: When receiving brawl or melee damage, the user can make a Rage Check to deal superficial damage equal to their Glory back on the attacker. This is granted by a hedgehog-spirit. The damage dealt cannot surpass the amount of damage received from the initial attack.

traits.name: Sense the True Form
traits.description: Determine if someone is something other than a mundane human through sense of smell. This is granted by a vulture-spirit. If they have not met the creature before they may not identify the scent. The Gift can be used in any form.

traits.name: Indomitable Spirit
traits.description: Ignore the effects of injury. The Gift user ignores physical impairment penalties upon activation.

traits.name: Beast's Fealty
traits.description: Command the loyalty of a single animal. This is granted by a falcon or lion-spirit of law. This lasts until until the Garou releases the animal or it dies, this can only be used on one animal at a time.

traits.name: Command the Gathering
traits.description: Draw the attention of those within hearing and viewing distance, regardless if they speak the same language. This is granted by a bird-of-paradise-spirit or another eye catching spirit. Add the Garou's Glory to their dice pools to any public speech/presentation with the Skills Persuasion or Performance. Cannot be used in combat and breaks when any physical altercation begins.

gift_attributes.duration: One scene or until it is broken or lapses

traits.name: Fangs of Judgment
traits.description: Bolster their allies claws and fangs against a single enemy who has previously harmed the pack. This is granted by any number of predator-spirits. A successful Gift test at Difficulty 3, grants the pack a +1 damage against the target. This can only be used one target and cannot stack by others using the same Gift.

gift_attributes.duration: Until the target dies or the sentence is revoked.

traits.name: Scent of the Past
traits.description: By smelling an object, the Garou can determine events that unfolded in the area. This is granted by a crow-spirit or other spirits that thrive in multiple environments. The Difficulty is determined by the information sought, with each point of margin revealing more information. This can be used in lupus form.

traits.name: Calcify
traits.description: Make a physical feature more difficult to open, destroy, or otherwise bypass. Cannot affect a material beyond a radius of one meter.

traits.name: Geas
traits.description: Command another to perform a task, but it cannot be used to extract information. Commands that would result in direct harm automatically fail. This is granted by a weaver-spirit and spirits that observed rigid hierarchies. Other supernatural creatures or those aware of the Garou can resist the command, or if it goes against their nature or would cause harm those they love.

gift_attributes.duration: Completion of task, scene ends, or geas is lifted.

traits.name: Oathbreaker's Bane
traits.description: Bind another to a promise, one that they understand and accept. This is granted by a elephant or snake-spirit. The oath giver receives a bonus to track down the oathbreaker equal to their Honor.

gift_attributes.duration: Indefinite or until oath is broken

traits.name: Strength of Purpose
traits.description: Roll one die for each point of Honor and heal one point of Superficial Willpower damage for each success or one of Aggravated Willpower per two success. This is granted by a stone or fire-spirit. This Gift can only be used once per session. This Gift can be used in any form.

traits.name: Take the True Form
traits.description: Force a shapeshifting creature to return to its "natural" form. This is granted by a river-spirit. This Gift can be used in homid or lupus form.

gift_attributes.duration: Margin number of turns or until user changes form

traits.name: Animal Magnetism
traits.description: Exudes a feral aura that draws the attention of humans. This is granted by a peacock-spirit or spirit associated with attraction. Grants Glory as bonus dice against humans with Social Skill tests.

traits.name: Howl of Assembly
traits.description: Heal a Willpower point of other Garou who join in the presence of the user, each margin increase the range by 100 meters. This is granted by a song-spirit or a spirit of an animal known for its unique call. This can only affect a character once per session. This Gift can be used in lupus form.

traits.name: Song of Rage
traits.description: The Garou can sing a song to increase nearby packmates rage by 1. Two points of Rage are given on a critical win. Characters can only be affected by this once per session.

traits.name: Song of Serenity
traits.description: The Garou can sing a song to decrease nearby packmates rage by 1. Two points of Rage are lost on a critical win with a minimum of 1. This power can also help snap pack members out of frenzy. Characters can only be affected by this once per session.

traits.name: Howl of Despair
traits.description: Inflict Willpower damage on non-allies in earshot with a howl. Affects everyone in earshot except for the Gift user's pack. Can also be used in lupus form.

traits.name: Lullaby
traits.description: Force a target to fall asleep. The victim can be awoken as easily as normal sleep, unless a critical win was scored against them. Supernatural targets ignore this critical condition and can resist the Gift entirely by spending Willpower.

gift_attributes.duration: Until awoken or the scene ends

traits.name: Call the Ridden
traits.description: Attract creatures possessed by spirits. This is granted by any spirit with the ability to posses. Those unable to resist will make every effort to reach the user, but will not go through any obvious hazards.

traits.name: Eyes of the Cobra
traits.description: Draw in a victim as if they are in a trance. This is granted by a snake-spirit. Those who are aware of the Garou or supernatural creatures can attempt to resist. Any violence towards the victim or other life threatening situations break the trance.

traits.name: Song of Inspiration
traits.description: The Garou can sing a song to buff either a Mental or Social Skill for their packmates. On a win, other members of the pack gain a bonus die for tests using that Skill, a critical win increases the bonus to two dice.

traits.name: Against the Odds
traits.description: Restore superficial Willpower of the Gift user and pack. This is granted by a warthog or badger-spirit. Each success of the test heals one Superficial Willpower. This can only be used once per story.

traits.name: Break the Shackles
traits.description: Break mind control or coercion from another supernatural. This is granted by any winged spirit, or spirits associated with Gorgon. The Storyteller can allow the victim to add their Resolve to the Gift test pool if they are actively trying to resist as well.

traits.name: Defy Death
traits.description: Rapidly heal when they are fully filled with Aggravated damage. This is granted by a wolf or bear-spirit. Each success heals one level of Health damage. This can only be used once per session and can be used in any form.

traits.name: Dreamwalk
traits.description: Use a spirit to enter another's dreams, leaving messages to scare them or better understand them. This is granted by a dream-spirit. If the attempt is to scare the victim, they gain no Willpower and lose an additional Willpower for each success of margin. This Gift can also be used in any form.

traits.name: Halt the Coward's Flight
traits.description: Those attempting to flee have their movement reduced to walking speed and airborne targets brought down to the ground. This is granted by a bog-spirit. This generally works on any mundane victim, whereas supernatural creatures can attempt to resist as determined by the Storyteller.

traits.name: Rapid Shift
traits.description: Rapidly shapeshift with a risk for damage. This is granted by any Gaian spirit. The Difficulty is dependent on the form; homid is 1, glabro and hispo is 2, and crinos is 3. Minor action penalty is removed, this can be used in any form.

traits.name: Razor Claws
traits.description: Grow supernaturally sharp claws. This is granted by a predatory animal spirit. This is free and instant for crinos and deal damage equal to half their Glory (rounded up).

traits.name: Sense Danger
traits.description: Add the users Wisdom to any attempt to detect traps, ambushes or other surprise attacks. This is granted by a prey animal spirit. This should only be added when failure will result in immediate danger.

traits.name: Tug the Leash
traits.description: Gain control over oneself while frenzying and leave frenzy more easily. The Gift user can make a test to choose a target to attack, but must still attack someone.

traits.name: Snarl of Challenge
traits.description: Reduce attackers pools by two dice when they attack anyone but the user. Gain additional Health equal to the Honor upon success. This is granted by a bear-spirit. The additional Health only absorbs one point of Superficial damage each. They do not protect against Aggravated damage.

traits.name: True Fear
traits.description: Instill fear to drive the victim away or terror that freezes the victim in place. This is granted by spirits of Fear. Mundane victims are automatically affected, supernatural creatures and those aware of the Garou may resist. Supernatural may shake off the effect after one turn by spending one point of Willpower.

traits.name: Wind Claws
traits.description: Ignore armor and damage reduction by an amount equal to the user's Wisdom. This is granted by a wind-spirit. This is instant and free for crinos form.

traits.name: Light of the Moon
traits.description: Glow brightly enough to blind anyone looking directly at oneself and damage nearby vampires. Ends early if the Gift user is incapacitated or chooses to end the Gift.

traits.name: Closing the Gap
traits.description: Move long distances to close the gap between them and an enemy. This is granted by a grasshopper or mantis-spirit. The amount covered is equal to 3 meters times their total Glory. They cannot go through things not physically possible to traverse.

traits.name: Kiss of Helios
traits.description: Take no damage from fire and reduce damage from stronger sources by the Glory per turn. This is granted by a sun ray-spirit. Anyone who strikes barehanded takes Superficial damage equal to the user's Glory.

traits.name: Luna's Armor
traits.description: Turn Aggravated damage from fire or silver into Superficial. This is granted by a Lune. The amount turned is equal to their Honor, but it is not halved.

traits.name: Silver Claws
traits.description: Turn the Garou's claws into silver to deal massive damage to other Garou. This is granted by a spite-spirit or elemental spirit of silver. This is free and instant for crinos form.

traits.name: Catfeet
traits.description: Automatically succeed any test to keep their balance, as long as it can hold their weight. This is granted by a cat-spirit. This gift can be activated reflexively if surprised with a Wits + Survival test Difficulty 3. Can be used in any form.

traits.name: An Exchange with Luna
traits.description: Send one's Rage away, to be summoned back later. Regain all Rage lost this way with a Resolve + Honor test at Difficulty 3, while one can see the moon. Can be used in any form.

traits.name: Eyes of the Owl
traits.description: Grants the ability to see in complete darkness. This is granted by an owl- or cat-spirit. Should the source be supernatural, they can their Wisdom as bonus dice if there is a resistance pool.

traits.name: Hare's Leap
traits.description: Leap great distances horizontally or vertically. This is granted by a leaping animal spirit. For every success on the test, they leap 3 meters.

traits.name: The Howl Carries
traits.description: Have a howl-based message travel further by being repeated by ordinary wolves. Garou and some spirits can notice the unusual nature of the relayed howl, but not humans. The effect can be contained to the bawn of one's caern by making a Rage Check.

traits.name: Penumbral Senses
traits.description: This allows the Garou to perceive both the physical realm and the Umbra at the same time without penalty. This is granted by a pattern spider. The test is performed against the Difficulty of the local Gauntlet (2-5 generally).

traits.name: Raging Strike
traits.description: Garou channel their Rage into their strikes. Gain a dice bonus equal to their Glory on a single Brawl attack. This is granted by a dire wolf-spirit.

traits.name: Resist Toxin
traits.description: Become more resistant to poisons, diseases, drugs, and similar toxic substances. A test to resist can be made against an appropriate Difficulty if one would not normally occur. Recreational drugs can be resisted by making a Rage Check and rolling against Difficulty 2.

traits.name: Shredding Skin
traits.description: Sacrifice health instead of Rage to change form. Associated with the Wyrm. The pain that this Gift inflicts does not grant Rage.

traits.name: Staredown
traits.description: When used on a single human or animal, they get out of the Garou's way while cowering from them. Succeeds automatically on unaware mortals. This is granted by a legendary wolf-spirit. When used against supernatural creatures or mortals aware of the Garou, a test must be made. A subject can be targeted by this power only once per scene and itcannot be used during physical combat. This can be used in any form.

traits.name: Bestial Regression
traits.description: Make a touched victim unable to communicate in any way beyond simple grunts and growls. Humans rationalize being affected by this Gift due to the Delirium, while supernatural beings can ignore it with Willpower but suffer a penalty to social rolls for the remainder. Can be used in any form.

traits.name: Bite of the Lycan
traits.description: Induce violent impulses in a bitten human. Associated with the Wyrm. The effects of this Gift intensify with each passing night, with the victim only fully succumbing from the third night onwards.

gift_attributes.duration: One night per margin of success

traits.name: Gnaw
traits.description: Gain a bonus to chew through any physical barrier or restraint. Can be used in crinos, hispo or lupus form.

traits.name: Sharpened Senses
traits.description: Heightens the werewolf's senses. While in Glabro they gain the senses of awolf, whereas in crinos, hispo or lupus sharpen to that of a human. This is granted by a wolf-spirit. The Difficulty of all Awareness tests are lowered by the character's Wisdom, minimum of 2. This can be used in any form.

traits.name: Spirit of the Fray
traits.description: The Garou can attack additional opponents within range, up to the character's Glory without a penalty. This is granted by an wolverine- or badger-spirit. A single attack roll is made an applied to every opponent engaged and covered by the cost. Any other bonuses of the attack apply to all opponents.

traits.name: Thwarting the Arrow
traits.description: Grant a bonus equal to their Honor to all test to avoid projectile weapons. This is granted by a Weaver vector-spirit. This Gift can only be used in crinos form.

traits.name: Urban Hunter
traits.description: Gain a bonus to hunting a certain quarry while within an urban setting. Associated with the Wyrm.

traits.name: Body Shift
traits.description: Finetunethe shape of physical body to become stronger, smaller, or more dense without changing forms completely. This is granted by a water-spirit. The Gift test is made at Difficulty 2. For each success of the margin they can redistribute one dot from one Physical Attribute to another. No Attribute can be reduced below 0 or taken above 5. Up to the Storytellers discretion, this Gift also allows the user to fit into small spaces, with the Difficulty based on the dimensions of the space.

traits.name: Jam Technology
traits.description: Temporarily wreak havoc on technology so that it no longer works. Once the duration expires, its normal operation resumes. This is granted by a Wyld spirit of discord. The Gift test is madeagainst a Difficulty as determined by the device complexity level, all equipment within 20 meters stops working based on the number of successes. It is possible to take out a wider range of devices than intended. Refer to the chart on page 148 of the Corebook.

traits.name: Tongue of the Beasts
traits.description: Communicate and speak freely with any animal as if they were a person, only limited by the animal's perspective and mind. This is granted by a lion- or moose-spirit. They can be convinced to aid in a task or yield information withthe Difficulties ranging from 2 to 5 depending on how close it is to mammals or how dire the information/task is.

traits.name: Gremlins
traits.description: A device within sight and five meters can be rendered useless, if the result is a critical win the device becomes inoperable and beyond repair. This is granted by a havoc-spirit. The difficulty of this test is determined by the device complexity level, refer to page 150 of the Corebook for the chart. This gift can also be used in lupus form.

traits.name: Corrupted Memories
traits.description: Replace oneself with someone else in the recent memories of a victim. Associated with the Wyrm. Alters one scene's worth of memories, and can only be used on humans who have no experience with or knowledge of the Garou.

traits.name: Jump Scare
traits.description: Show a victim a brief glimpse of the depths of the Umbra to terrify them. The victim can resist this Gift's effects by spending Willpower, but then cannot spend Willpower to reroll dice. Can be used in any form.

traits.name: Hungry Teeth
traits.description: Turn one's teeth more dangerous against spirits and fomori and regain Willpower from killing supernatural entities. Associated with the Wyrm. Free and instantaneous in crinos form.

traits.name: Banish Spirit
traits.description: The Garou can send present spirits possessing a host on the same realm back to the Umbra. This is granted by a Incarna avatar. The spirit can cause Aggravated damage to it's host equal to Power minus the margin of the test.

traits.name: Ghost Walk
traits.description: Turn intangible to pass through a solid surface. The state of intangibility only applies to inorganic surfaces, so this Gift cannot be used to avoid grapples or attacks.

traits.name: Ancestral Conviction
traits.description: Gain their Honor as bonus dice on Persuasion Skill tests against other Garou. This is granted by a legendary wolf-spirit. This can be used in any form.

traits.name: Wyrm-Speech
traits.description: Damage spirits and Talismans with a roar or chant. Associated with the Wyrm. Critical successes can deal partial Aggravated damage or permanently destroy Talismans.

traits.name: Cutting Words
traits.description: Inflict social and mental impairment on a target in a conversation, along with visible blood loss. Associated with the Wyrm. The inflicted blood loss only causes actual damage on a Brutal Outcome. Aside from the blood, the conversation appears normal to those without supernatural senses.

gift_attributes.duration: One turn per point of margin

traits.name: Song of Valor
traits.description: The Garou can sing a song to buff their packmates Willpower rerolls. Willpower rerolls can roll any amount of dice, rather than just 3. It cannot be used to reroll Brutal results. This Gift can only be used once per session.

traits.name: Mouth Full of Teeth
traits.description: Fill one's mouth with deadly but fragile additional fangs. Associated with the Wyrm. Every bite attack reduces the damage bonus by 1, and once it reaches 0 the Gift user cannot make any bite attacks until they rest in their breed form.

gift_attributes.duration: One scene or until the fangs' damage becomes 0

traits.name: Primal Anger
traits.description: Inflict one Aggravated Damage to a victim and gain Rage. This is granted by a legendary wolf-spirit. The amount of Rage gained is dependent on the number of successes on a Glory test. This can only be used once per session.

traits.name: Curse of Aeolus
traits.description: Call upon a fog to cover the area. This is granted by a fog-spirit. Those in the fog suffer a negative two dice penalty to Awareness and Firearms tests. This Gift can also be used in any form.

traits.name: Coup de Grâce
traits.description: Add the Glory rating into their next attack to end the opponent in one hit. This is granted by any spirit associated with pride. Only works if the damage would reduce the opponents Health to 0.

gift_attributes.duration: Next Brawl or Melee attack, or the scene ends

traits.name: Kali's Scar
traits.description: Any damage dealt by these claws cannot be healed even with supernatural means. This is granted by a rot-spirit. The user also gains bonus dice equal to their Glory in attempts to track or locate the victim while the Gift is active.

traits.name: Wasp Talons
traits.description: Make ranged attacks with their claws. This is granted by a wasp-spirit. Gain one Superficial damage from their claws being damaged and cannot use claw attacks until this is healed.

traits.name: Gorgon's Visage
traits.description: Paralyze someone by making eye contact. This is granted by an avatar of Gorgon. Those who break free cannot be paralyzed again for the remainder of the scene.

traits.name: Odious Aroma
traits.description: Stop others from coming near the Garou by releasing a horrible stench. This is granted by a skunk-spirit. This Gift can only be applied outside of combat. This Gift can also be used in any form.

traits.name: Face in the Crowd
traits.description: Disappear into a crowd to shake off pursuers, adding their Honor rating to the Difficulty rating to find them. This is granted by a roach-spirit. This can only be used in homid form or lupus form, depending on which group they are blending into.

traits.name: Streets Tell Stories
traits.description: Listen in on nearby conversations by placing their ear to the ground. This is granted by a city-spirit. Upon success the conversation can be heard as if they were there, but it does not pick up ambient sounds such as cars or the other end of a phone conversation. This Gift can also be used in any form.

traits.name: A Thousand Eyes
traits.description: See through the eyes of any animal nearby as long as they are cockroach sized are larger. This is granted by an ant-spirit. Should the animal be killed while the Gift is being used, the user suffers one point of Aggravated Willpower damage.

traits.name: Between the Cracks
traits.description: Disappear and reappear through a crack that is the result from wear or damage. This is granted by a spirit of shadow, decay, or ruin. The Garou must be able to see both cracks.

traits.name: Brother's Scent
traits.description: Blend into a group as long as their form matches those around them. This is granted by an avatar of Unicorn. This Gift only works in Homid form unless it's a Garou gathering, this also doesn't prevent them from being noticed for acting unfit to the situation or if the person looking already knows them by sight.

traits.name: Calm the Furious Beast
traits.description: Bring another supernatural creature out of Frenzy. This is granted by a legendary wolf spirit. The Gift test is made against the target's current Rage or Difficulty 3 for other supernaturals. This Gift can also be used in any form.

traits.name: Reveal Trauma
traits.description: Learn of a targets greatest physical, mental, or supernatural ailment. This is granted by a spirit of suffering. Any attempt to help heal or repair gains a two-dice bonus. This Gift can also be used in any form.

traits.name: Life's Presence
traits.description: The user becomes aware of all mundane life as well as any supernatural. This is granted by a spirit of the soil. Only those that feign a living body can be detected, either through possessing a body or feigning life.

traits.name: Share the Pain
traits.description: Take away half of the physical damage a packmate would receive and the user takes it as either as Health or Willpower. This is granted by a bee-spirit. The Garou picks a number of packmates equal to their Wisdom. Any defensive Gifts used by the user apply as normal.

traits.name: Camouflage
traits.description: Blend into the wilderness. This is granted by a deer-spirit. By spending a full turn and 1 Willpower they can increase this to their entire pack, should they move or make any sound it breaks. This Gift can also be used in any form.

traits.name: Lacerating Wind
traits.description: Target a group of foes with powerful wind that reduces their physical attack pools by 2. This is granted by an avatar of the north wind. It can also be used against a single target to knock them off their feet or off whatever they are standing on.

traits.name: Chill Cloak
traits.description: Add their Honor rating to any Stealth, Survival, or Larceny attempt when avoiding heat based, movement based or light based sensors. This is granted by a alligator-spirit or other cold-blooded predatory spirit. By spending a full turn and 1 Willpower they can extended this effect to all packmates nearby.

traits.name: Blood of the Wastes
