traits.description: The user becomes immune everything but the most extreme environmental conditions. They are able to travel for long periods of time without shelter. This is granted by hardly or hibernating spirit such as a bear or turtle-spirit. Add their Honor rating to any tests of Survival to find food or water, also reduce environmental penalties by their Honor rating. This Gift can also be used in any form.

traits.name: Claws of Frozen Death
traits.description: Create claws that when they hit someone they receive a two-dice penalty to defend or dodge future attacks. This is granted by a ice or winter-spirit. This is instant and free in Crinos form.

traits.name: Augur
traits.description: Look into a reflective surface and see through another reflective surface in another area. This is granted by a water-spirit or spirit associated with knowledge. They cannot hear or smell what is going on, only able to see. This Gift can also be used in any form.

traits.name: Blackout
traits.description: Extinguish one light source within eyesight per dot of Wisdom. This is granted by a spirit associated with darkness or are nocturnal animal. Those who cannot see in the dark and/or do not have a light source are subject to a penalty. This Gift can be used in any form.

traits.name: Serpent's Coil
traits.description: Summon a snake like fog to restrict opponents. This is granted by a constrictor snake-spirit. The serpents share three Health levels and use the user's Wits + Wisdom to avoid and take attacks, but can only be attacked with fire or supernatural attacks that hurt the incorporeal.

gift_attributes.duration: One scene until ended or destroyed

traits.name: Hands of the Earth
traits.description: Move large or small objects at a distance. This is granted by a earth or stone-spirit. The Garou must concentrate for the full turn to use the Gift and do nothing else while using it.

traits.name: Shrouded Aspect
traits.description: The Garou becomes invisible to onlookers and conventional cameras. This is granted by a smoke-spirit. The Garou can not move faster than a walking pace or it will break.

gift_attributes.duration: A number of turns equal to the Wisdom of the user

traits.name: Skinbind
traits.description: Transform mundane items into a tattoo-like image on their skin, the items cannot be too heavy for them to lift and the weight is still felt. This is granted by a moth-spirit or a spirit associated with drawing/writing. Store an amount of objects up to their Wisdom rating and non-mundane may come with side effects. Storing the item takes a full turn but retrieving it is a minor action.

traits.name: Energize
traits.description: Power any object as if it was plugged in or fueled up. This Gift does not repair destroyed items and does not equate them having knowledge to use it.

traits.name: Control Machine
traits.description: Issue simple commands to machines. This is granted by a machine-spirit. The commands cannot harm the machine.

traits.name: Doppelganger
traits.description: Take on the likeness of another humanoid or wolf. This is granted by a shadow or mockingbird-spirit. The Gift user must know the subject being copied by face. This Gift can also be used in any form.

traits.name: Recover Memory
traits.description: Retrieve damaged or destroyed information from an object. This is granted by a data-spirit. This Gift does not give the ability to understand the material, additional tests may be required. This Gift can also be used in any form.

traits.name: Sacred Boundary
traits.description: Create a boundary that when broken while still active gives the user a glimpse of the movement. This is granted by a dog-spirit. This lasts either for a day and a night unless the objects anchoring it are moved/destroyed. This Gift can also be used in any form.

gift_attributes.duration: A day or a night or until broken

traits.name: Blessed Brew
traits.description: Infuse a drink so that it increases resistance to fear or supernatural mental abilities by adding the Gift users Glory to the resistance pool . This is granted by a hearth or herb-spirit. This Gift can only be used once per session and can also be used in any form.

traits.name: Territorial Dominance
traits.description: The user gains a bonus die on marked territory. This is granted by a lion-spirit or other feral cat-spirit. Damage to the territory gives Willpower damage to the user.

gift_attributes.duration: A number of nights equal to the user's Glory

traits.name: Balor's Gaze
traits.description: Look upon someone and cause horrible paint with a 2 dice penalty to all tests. This is granted by a pain-spirit or spirit of age and time. Only one subject can be affected at any given time.

traits.name: The Living Wood
traits.description: Use nature to protect themselves, anyone attempting to harm them must deal with grapple attacks from nearby trees and plants. This is granted by a Glade Child. If there are no suitable plants or trees the gift fails.

traits.name: Hidden Killer
traits.description: Cover up how someone died. This is granted by a beetle or spider-spirit. Mundane attempts to gather information fail, those familiar with the Garou can make the attempt but suffer a dice penalty equal to the Gift user's Honor.

traits.name: Render Down
traits.description: Rot or erode any inert material they touch. This is granted by a insect-spirit or a spirit associated with carrion. The process takes a few minutes, where the user must maintain contact with the object.

traits.name: Gaia's Embrace
traits.description: Pull those flying to the ground. This is granted by a rock and mountain-spirit. Maintaining this Gift requires the users' full attention and lapses when they perform another action or use another Gift.

traits.name: Quicksand
traits.description: Turn the ground into a sticky surface that gives a 2-dice penalty to all physical defense pools and reduces movement to a slow walking pace. This is granted by a earth elemental. This Gift can only be used on natural ground, not indoors or paved surfaces.

traits.name: Shield of the Wyld
traits.description: Ignore physical damage levels equal to the user's Honor each turn as long as it's caused by mundane weapons. This is granted by a Serpentine-spirit. Natural weapons such as claws and fangs are unaffected and while this is active the user cannot use tools or technological devices.

traits.name: Fatal Flaw
traits.description: Learn a method not yet known to deal Aggravated damage to a target, if there is nothing they gain a bonus die to attacks. This is granted by a termite-spirit. During the full turn studying they can do nothing else. This Gift can also be used in any form.

traits.name: Icy Chill of Despair
traits.description: Gain Glory as bonus dice for Intimidation tests. This is granted by a Stormcrow. Those nearby, other than the packmates, cannot reroll using Willpower or regain Willpower other than through mystical means.

traits.name: Dire Distraction
traits.description: Cause a distraction that makes the target lose dice equal to the user's Glory on their next action. This is granted by a shadow-spirit. A subject can only be affected by this Gift once per scene. This Gift can also be used in any form.

traits.name: Thunderclap
traits.description: Stun everyone within 20 meters. This is granted by a Incarna of Thunder. Those who pass receive a two-dice penalty to any action other than to defend for a turn, unless it's a critical. Everyone else is knocked to the ground.

traits.name: Under the Gun
traits.description: Touch someone and they can no longer use Gifts to dodge projectiles, attackers also gain two bonus dice against the victim with projectiles. This is granted by a raven-spirit. Can be removed by the Rite of Abjuration.

gift_attributes.duration: One day per dot of Glory

traits.name: Fetch Bounty
traits.description: Locate mundane nonliving objects. This is granted by a magpie-spirit. This Gift can also be used in any form.

traits.name: Speech of the World
traits.description: Read and speak any living language. This is granted by a parrot-spirit. This Gift can also be used in any form.

traits.name: Whispered Passage
traits.description: Muffle the natural sounds when moving. This is granted by a cat-spirit. This Gift can also be used in any form.

traits.name: Burrow
traits.description: Rapidly tunnel into the earth to get past obstacles or seek shelter. This is granted by a mole-spirit. The tunnels are unstable and only last one scene.

traits.name: The Golden Path
traits.description: Add the users Wisdom rating to test to navigate an area with static danger or obstacles. This is granted by a ant-spirit. This bonus can be shared with packmates if the user can communicate with them. This Gift can also be used in any form.

traits.name: Pack Instinct
traits.description: The Garou has instinctual knowledge of the whereabouts of their packmates. This is granted by a bee or other hive insect-spirit. They must be within a 10-kilometer radius. This Gift can also be used in any form.

traits.name: The Silver Compact
traits.description: Invoke a compact and receive protection from an otherwise hostile spirit. This is granted by a -spirit. This also allows the user to ignore Health or Willpower damage equal to their Honor rating each turn. This Gift can also be used in any form.

traits.name: Blood of the Pack
traits.description: Members of the pack can make Rage Checks for one another. This is granted by a wasp-spirit. The total amount of Rage Checks able to be made this way are equal to the Gift user's Honor. It cannot be used if Unity of the Pack has been used in the same scene. This Gift can be used in any form.

traits.name: Unity of the Pack
traits.description: Members of the pack can spend Willpower for one another. This is granted by any familial spirit. The total amount of Willpower spent this way is equal to the Gift user's Honor. It cannot be used if Blood of the Pack has been used in the same scene. This Gift can be used in any form.

traits.name: Luna's Avenger
traits.description: Gain a bonus of one die for each Impaired packmate and two for each member with their Health tracker filled with Aggravated damage for all physical combat attacks and defense tests. This is granted by a Lune. The bonus dice cannot exceed the Gift user's Honor.

traits.name: Path of Blood
traits.description: Almost every Tremere studies the Path of Blood as her primary path. It encompasses some of the most fundamental principles of Thaumaturgy, based as it is on the manipulation of Kindred vitae. If a player wishes to select another path as her character's primary path, she'd better have a good reason (though choosing a different path is by no means unheard of).

traits.name: Thaumaturgical Countermagic
traits.description: This is less of a path than it is a separate Discipline, as the power to resist Thaumaturgy can be taught independently of Thaumaturgy, even to those Kindred who are incapable of mastering even the simplest ritual. However, the techniques of Thaumaturgical Countermagic are not officially taught outside Clan Tremere, for obvious reasons. Any non-Tremere who displays the ability to resist Thaumaturgy quickly becomes the subject of potentially fatal scrutiny from the entirety of Clan Tremere.
traits.system:
Thaumaturgical Countermagic is treated as a separate Discipline. It cannot be taken as a character's primary path, and a rating in it does not allow the character to perform rituals. The use of Thaumaturgical Countermagic is treated as a free action in combat and does not require a split dice pool. To oppose a Thaumaturgy power or ritual, a character must have a Thaumaturgical Countermagic rating equal to or greater than the rating of that power or ritual (which means that rituals of Level Six or greater cannot ever be countered with this power). The player spends a blood point and rolls the number of dice indicated by the character's Thaumaturgical Countermagic rating (difficulty equal to the difficulty of the power in use). Each success cancels one of the opposing thaumaturge's successes.

Thaumaturgical Countermagic can only be used against Tremere Thaumaturgy at full effectiveness. It works with halved dice pools against non-Tremere blood magic and mortal 'hedge wizardry', and is completely ineffective against non-vampiric magics and powers. Thaumaturgical Countermagic can be learned by characters who are unable to learn Thaumaturgy (e.g. those with the Merit Magic Resistance). Any non-Tremere character with a rating in this power automatically gains the Flaw Clan Enmity (Tremere), receiving no freebie points for it. This power cannot be taken during character creation and cannot be spontaneously developed. It costs the same as any other non-clan Discipline to learn.

traits.name: Spirit Manipulation
traits.description: Not to be confused with, or perhaps derived from the ancient Path of Spirit Thaumaturgy (Dark Ages Companion, p. 107) Spirit Manipulation is a relatively recent innovation of the Tremere. Created to replace the rituals practiced by the clan in the days when it was a band of mortal wizards, Spirit Manipulation is the art of forcing spirits into actions and situations that would normally be anathema to them. Spirit Manipulation simulates many effects that can be created by Lupine, and shamanic mages, but does so by forcing the spirits involved into a grotesque mockery of their normal behaviors. Any botch with Spirit Manipulation not only inflicts the normal loss of Will power involved with thaumaturgical mishaps but also turns the full force of the spirit's wrath against the offending vampire.

traits.name: Weather Control
traits.description: Command over the weather has long been a staple power of wisards both mortal and immortal, and this path is said by many to predate the Tremere by millennia. The proliferation of usage of this path outside the clan tends to confirm this theory; Weather Control is quite common outside the Tremere, and even outside the Camarilla. Lower levels of this path allow subtle manipulations, while higher stages of mastery allow a vampire to call up raging storms. The area affected by this power is usually rather small, no more than three or four miles in diameter, and the changes the wreaks are not always immediate.
traits.system:
The number of successes rolled indicates how long it takes the weather magic to take effect. One success indicates an entire day may pass before the weather changes to the thaumaturge's liking, while a roll with five successes brings an almost instant effect.

The difficulty of the Willpower roll necessary to invoke this power may change depending on the current local weather conditions and the weather the character is attempting to create. The Storyteller should impose a bonus (-1 or -2 difficulty) for relatively minor shifts, such as clearing away a light drizzle or calling lightning when a severe thunderstorm is already raging. Conversely, a penalty (+1 or +2 difficulty) should be applied when the desired change is at odds with the current conditions, such as summoning the same light drizzle in the middle of the Sahara Desert or calling down lightning from a cloudless sky.

If the character tries to strike a specific target with lightning, the player must roll Perception + Occult (difficulty 6 if the target is standing in open terrain, 8 if he is under shelter, or 10 if he inside but near a window) in addition to the base roll to use Thaumaturgy. Otherwise the bolt goes astray, with the relative degree of failure of the roll determining where exactly the lightning strikes.

traits.name: The Lure of Flames
traits.description: This path grants the thaumaturge the ability to conjure forth mystical flames - small fires at first, but skilled magicians may create great conflagrations. The Lure of Flames is greatly feared, as fire is one of the surest ways to bring Final Death upon a vampire.
traits.system: The number of successes determines how accurately the thaumaturge places the flame in his desired location. One success is all that is necessary to conjure a flame in one's hand, while five successes place a flame anywhere in the Kindred's line of sight.

traits.name: Movement of the Mind
traits.description: This path gives the thaumaturge the ability to move objects telekinetically through the mystic power of blood. At higher levels, even flight is possible (but be careful who sees you…). Objects under the character's control may be manipulated as if she held them - they may be lifted, spun, juggled or even 'thrown,' though creating enough force to inflict actual damage requires mastery of the fourth level or greater. Some thaumaturges skilled in this path even use it to guard their havens, animating swords, axes and firearms to ward off intruders. This path may frighten and disconcert onlookers. Many people are quite put off when the pages of a book turn by themselves!
traits.system: The number of successes indicates the duration of the thaumaturge's control over the object (or subject). Each success allows one turn of manipulation, though the Kindred may attempt to maintain control after this time by making a new roll (she need not spend additional blood to maintain control). If the roll is successful, control is maintained. If a thaumaturge loses or relaxes control over an object and later manipulates it again, her player must spend another blood point, as a new attempt is being made. If this power is used to manipulate a living being, the subject may attempt to resist. In this case, the thaumaturge and the subject make opposed Willpower rolls each turn the control is exercised.

traits.name: The Path of Conjuring
traits.description: Invoking objects 'out of thin air' has been a staple of occult and supernatural legend since long before the rise of the Tremere. This Thaumaturgical path enables powerful conjurations limited only by the mind of the practitioner. Objects summoned via this path bear two distinct characteristics. They are uniformly 'generic' in that each object summoned, if summoned again, would look exactly as it did at first. For example, a knife would be precisely the same knife if created twice; the two would be indistinguishable. Even a specific knife - the one a character's father used to threaten her - would appear identical every time it was conjured. A rat would have repeated 'tiled' patterns over its fur, and a garbage can would have the exact same fluted texture over its surface. Additionally, conjured objects bear no flaws: Weapons have no dents or scratches, tools have no distinguishing marks, and computers have featureless casings.
traits.system:
The limit on the size of conjured objects appears to be that of the conjurer; nothing larger than the thaumaturge can be created. The conjurer must also have some degree of familiarity with the object he wishes to call forth. Simply working from a picture or imagination calls for a higher difficulty, while objects with which the character is intimately familiar (such as the knife described above) may actually lower the difficulty, at the Storyteller's discretion.

When a player rolls to conjure something, the successes gained on the roll indicate the quality of the summoned object. One success yields a shoddy, imperfect creation, while five successes garner the thaumaturge a nearly perfect replica.

traits.name: Hands of Destruction
traits.description: This Path is practiced almost exclusively by the thaumaturges of the Sabbat. Though it is not widely seen outside that sect, a few Camarilla Tremere have managed to learn the secrets of this path over the centuries. The Hands of Destruction has an infamous history, and some Tremere refuse to practice it due to rumors that it is demonic in origin. Brutal and painful, this path provides thaumaturges with offensive capabilities not found in other, less martial paths. It embodies the violent nature of its Sabbat wielders, existing solely to cause entropy and decay.

traits.name: Oneiromancy
traits.description: Although the corrupt dream-magic of the Path of Morpheus had been used by the Sabbat for decades, the original augury that it was based upon has been a long-held secret by the Tremere. Oneiromancy, divination rooted in the somnolent state of consciousness, is actually based upon ancient Greek philosophies of prophecy. The Tremere, adopting these ancient sortileges, developed them into the thaumaturgical path of Oneiromancy shortly after their transformation to vampiric magi. With the recent loss of the Sabbat's Tremere antitribu, the Path of Morpheus has since fallen from common knowledge, and Oneiromancy no longer carries the stigma once associated with its practitioners. With the approaching of Gehenna, the elders of Clan Tremere have again re-thought their disdain of Oneiromancy in the clan in an effort to gain more information on unfolding events.
traits.system: Use of any of these powers requires at least five minutes of reading the signs during which the Warlock goes into a trance similar to sleep. Awareness of his surroundings is limited, and he can 'awaken' from the trance at any time but will suffer the same penalties as if awakening during the day for three turns (maximum dice pool equals his Humanity score). Oneiromancy powers can also be used while the thaumaturge is in torpor.

traits.name: Mastery Of The Mortal Shell
traits.description: A thaumaturge practicing Mastery of the Mortal Shell explores the fundamental functions of the body, granting control over the physical workings of a subject. Lesser powers of this path are clumsy in their control of the body, but become increasingly precise and complete. In the early nights, Mastery of the Mortal Shell was created to assist in the hunting the Tzimisce and Gangrel enemies of the Tremere, but its uses were expanded to enforce subservience among enslaved Gargoyles after their first revolt. The powers of this path form the foundation for many tales of witches' curses and possession in folklore.
traits.system: Unless otherwise stated, the powers of Mastery of the Mortal Shell last the number of turns equal to the number of successes a thaumaturge scores on his activation roll.

traits.name: Alchemy
traits.description:
Thaumaturgy has some of it's roots in the early practices of Hermetic alchemy, which concerned itself with changing the properties of given ideals. Indeed, much of Thaumaturgy's codified structure and basic principles are very similar too those of alchemy.

In the near-millennium that has passed since the Tremere's earliest efforts with blood magic, alchemy has fallen by the wayside, however. Modern technology makes possible almost anything this path can do and far more - it is easier and more reliable to mine for gold, for example, than it is to create it by transmuting other materials. Still old habits die hard among vampires, and the Tremere are no exception. A few elders still practice this path, and of these, some still teach it to their childer to illustrate the basic tenets of modern blood magic.
traits.system:
The number of successes on the activation roll determines how radically a thaumaturge may alter a given compound's form. He need not use all his successes - a thaumaturge wishing to effect only a simple change in form need not turn lead into gold if he accumulates five successes. The thaumaturge must, however, acquire at least the minimum number of successes suggested by the table below to generate the effects he seeks.
Changing one element into another requires familarity with both elements. One cannot suddenly decide he wants to change a balloon's worth of helium into a sphere of solid titanium if he has never dealt with either of those elements before. Simply knowing an atomic number isn't enough to warp the laws of nature so radically. It is worthwhile to note, however, that Alchemy makes no distinction between naturally occurring and man-made elements - it affects Einsteinium as readily as it affects carbon.

Note also that this path has fallen out of practice because it is less useful in modern nights than it was in the past. Transmutations create Hermetic 'ideals' rather than perfect real-world resources which cannot be broken down into lesser constructs. A hunk of gold is a hunk of gold - an attempt to cast coins with it will reduce the hunk to a pile of inert sludge. Before you ask - yes, this means your Tremere anarch will not be able to create nuclear bombs by turning the prince's shoes into uranium. There's nothing wrong with converting lead into gold and selling that hunk of gold to someone else, however - caveat emptor!

traits.name: Biothaumaturgy
traits.description: This bizarre path reputedly has its roots in the secret tomes of the inscrutable Black Hand. Over the past few years, its esoteric secrets have spread among certain cabals of Tremere, who regard it as a curious mixture of Hermetic science and genetics. Biothaumaturgy concerns itself with the manipulation of life energies. In the laboratories of inspired biothaumaturges may be found strange creatures almost out of myth, veritable Frankenstein's monsters and less wholesome creations. Although it is not considered a 'taboo' path, Biothaumaturgy is nonetheless strange and unsettling to those who observe its results.
traits.system: Biothaumaturgy does not require the expenditure of blood points to create its magic. Rather, the powers of this path take one week per level to complete at Levels One and Two, and one month per level to complete at Levels Three through Five. For example, the Level One power takes one week to work, while the Level Three power takes three months for the biothaumaturge's experiments to come to fruition. Biothaumaturgy also requires the thaumaturge to have a laboratory where he may conduct his experiments. This need be nothing more complex than a doctor's table and a few sharp knives, but it may be as complex as the 'mad scientist' affairs of pulp fiction, with steaming alembics and crackling generators. The Storyteller should feel free to modify a character's Biothaumaturgy difficulties if he has an excellent laboratory resources or his facilities are utterly lacking. Working with a crude lab may add two to the difficulty while a high-grade facility may lower the difficulties by two.

traits.name: The Focused Mind
traits.description: Developed by a Tremere Kabbalah scholar, this little-known path of Thaumaturgy has been around for a few centuries and remains in practice by a select few. Practitioners of the Focused Mind greatly enhance their mental alacrity and readiness, allowing better comprehension of problems and situations. Thaumaturges, already disciplined of mind, become frightfully powerful in the realm of the cerebral when mastering this path.
traits.system: Unlike most Thaumaturgy, the powers of this path do not require an action to invoke, although they still require the normal blood expenditure and Willpower activation roll. Each of these powers is usable only once per turn.

traits.name: The Hearth Path
traits.description: For those Tremere who maintain private havens, secrecy is paramount. The Hearth Path seems to have evolved from a collection of rituals designed to protect the integrity of a vampire's sanctum sanctorum. Havens protected by these powers often acquire reputations as 'haunted' houses and the like. The Tremere are quick to point out, however, that these reputations help to strengthen the Masquerade rather than jeopardize it. When people find out that the house is just a house with a spooky atmosphere, they chastise themselves for being so foolish as to believe in ghosts.
traits.system: This Path functions only with the thaumaturge's primary haven; their home, as it were, which contains their sanctum sanctorum.

traits.name: Path of The Blood's Curse
traits.description:
Elder vampires have forever flaunted their powers over their lessers, the ancillae and neonates. With the approaching threat of Gehenna, young Kindred actively pursue ways to circumvent the stranglehold these powerful elders possess in the Jyhad. With this desire driving their ambitions, a few rogue Panders and Caitiff thaumaturges gathered together and developed this thaumaturgal path as a means of ousting those above them.

These rebels resorted to a very basic concept for this path of thaumaturgy by exposing the inherent disadvantages that accompany the vampiric Embrace. The elder' paranoia has only increased exponentially due to this movement toward a new method of usurping their power and vitae.

traits.name: Path Of Curses
traits.description: Mankind learned early in history to hate, envy, lust and wish malice upon itself. In such spitefulness curses were born. Most were just plain words and gestures, but some, spoken with such vehemence and spite, became tangible and formed true curses. In the dark depths of passionate hate, curses gained more weight, more substance, until people could use the evil eye or a spoken word to afflict such banes upon their fellow men. Early in the history of Thaumaturgy, the Path of Curses was developed and refined. It is still known by its early names in certain circles: Makficium, Hexen-Kraft, the Evil Eye. Victims of this path, often called accursed, are subject to extreme treatment and psychological feelings of inadequacy, often to the point of becoming social outcasts. When accursed are afflicted, friends and society tend to distance themselves from the victim, creating a social schism and driving the accursed toward alienation.
traits.system:
When cursing a victim, the thaumaturge is obvious - curses must be spoken aloud and directly toward the target of their ill effects, although the curse itself is often comprised of dead languages and sounds like gibberish. In modem, industrialized countries, many tend to discount the verbal telltales of being cursed, while some areas of the world might see a witch who acts so brazenly outright assaulted. Additionally, the thaumaturge requires some essence of the victim to curse him. This essence can be hair, blood, flesh or any other part of the victim. This link binds the mystic energies bringing the malison to the accursed.

Because of the severe psychological effects on a victim, the target may attempt to resist all the powers of this path with a Willpower roll (difficulty 7, unless stated otherwise). This is the victim asserting his own strength of will and personality to throw off the yoke of anathema. The thaumaturge can lift the curses that she brings down at any time she wishes, which makes an excellent bargaining chip.

traits.name: Path of Transmutation
traits.description: Thaumaturges practicing the art of Alchemy can manipulate the forms of solids, liquids and gases. Guns and knives melt into pools of metal, wood petrifies and becomes brittle, and common boundaries such as walls and doors transmute to vapor. The laws of chemistry do not bind Warlocks employing the powers of transmutation, as materials shift their state regardless of their temperature. Effects similar to those achieved by true alchemy can be achieved by using the Path of Transmutation. This path is looked upon somewhat unfavorably among the Tremere, as it reflects a 'quick and dirty' effect rather than true mastery of alchemical transmutation.

traits.name: The Vine of Dionysus
traits.description: Years before the rise of the mighty Roman Empire, pagan sects dedicated to the Greek god Dionysus thrived. Small cults of vampires worshiped the lord of rebirth as a release from the burdens of undeath. Symbolized in oinos, or wine, and orgy, the cults of Dionysus spread their intoxicating practices throughout Eastern Europe. In practice, members of the cult of Dionysus would gather at night and debauch themselves with oinos, orgies and other euphoric activities. When the ecstasy rose to fevered pitch, the rites might culminate in castration or even cannibalism. Any practitioner of the Vine of Dionysus often refers to himself/herself as a thyrsus (females are occasionally called 'Maenads', preferably from a safe distance). These Warlocks sometimes wear jewelry or other accouterments with ivy and/or pine cone motifs as symbols of their dedication.

traits.name: The Path of Technomancy
traits.description: The newest path to be accepted by the Tremere hierarchy as part of the clan's official body of knowledge, the Path of Technomancy is a relatively recent innovation. It was developed in the latter half of the 20th century, and has not yet spread far beyond the North American Pontifices. The path focuses on the control of electronic devices, from wristwatches to computers, and its proponents maintain that it is a prime example of the versatility of Thaumaturgy with regards to a changing world. More conservative Tremere state that mixing Tremere magic with mortal science borders on treason or even blasphemy, and some European Regents have gone so far as to declare knowledge of Technomancy grounds for expulsion from their chantries. The Inner Council did approve the introduction of the path into the clan's grimoires, but has yet to voice any opinion on the conservative opposition to Technomancy.

traits.name: Elemental Mastery
traits.description: This path allows a thaumaturge limited control over and communication with inanimate objects. Mistakenly believed by many to be related to the four basic elements (earth, air, fire, and water), this path is actually closer to an amalgamation of Spirit Thaumaturgy and The Path of Conjuring. Elemental Mastery can only be used to affect the unliving - a vampire couldn't cause a tree to walk by using Animate the Unmoving, for instance.

traits.name: Willpower
traits.description: Willpower is a tracker, with both a maximum rating and a temporary amount of points to be used. It represents the character's confidence and determination to accomplish a task.

traits.name: Humanity
traits.description: Represents how close a character remains to their human nature, to specific people vital to them, and how easily they slip away from human concerns and instead towards the whims of the Beast

traits.name: Blood Potency
traits.description: As time passes the kindred's blood thickens from age within the boundaries of their Generation's limit. While the stronger they become with age, there is a downside that it will take more blood to satisfy the vampire. Kindred generally raise Blood Potency by 1 every 100 years while active but even intense or dangerous experiences can expedite the process. On the reverse side, Kindred in torpor loses one point for every 50 years inactive. It can never rise nor lower beyond the cap determined by their Generation.

traits.name: Hunger
traits.description: The drive for vampires to feed, taking the place of every other drive, urge, and pleasure. Hunger calls to vampires constantly, whispering and screaming in equal measure of its need and desire for blood.

traits.name: Rage
traits.description: Rage is one of the most defining traits of the Garou. The hearts and souls of the Garou are consumed by Rage, and through it's manifestation is how they perform terrifying acts of brutality.

traits.name: Rite of Abjuration
traits.description: This Rite will purify a person, place, or object, driving out any spiritual possessions.

traits.name: Rite of Rage
traits.description: Increase the Rage of the participants.

traits.name: Rite of Tranquility
traits.description: Reduce the Rage of everyone participating.

traits.name: Rite of Contrition
traits.description: A Rite used to either make amends with a spirit or to remove chagrin.

traits.name: Rite of Forgetful Record
traits.description: This Rite is used to wreak havoc on any recorded information about the Garou.

traits.name: Rite of the Living Caern
traits.description: A Rite used to bring life to a caern, strengthening their bond and it's spiritual connection to the Umbra

traits.name: Rite of Shadow Passage
traits.description: This Rite allows a pack to open a pathway between the physical world and the Umbra.

traits.name: Rite of Dedication
traits.description: A Rite used to bind clothing to Garou, so that when they shapeshift their clothes do not rip off.

traits.name: Rite of Kinseeking
traits.description: A Rite Used to locate Kin before accidents occur.

traits.name: Rite of Spirit Summoning
traits.description: A Rite used to summon an absent, unaware, or unwilling spirit.

traits.name: Rite of Binding
traits.description: A Rite used to bind a spirit to a physical feature.

traits.name: Rite of Shame
traits.description: A Rite that is used as a form of punishment.

traits.name: Rite of Patronage
traits.description: A Rite used to adopt a Patron Spirit.
traits.pool: Renown (the type associated with the new Patron Spirit) + Etiquette

traits.name: Rite of Celebration
traits.description: A Rite used to celebrate victories.

traits.name: Rite of Caern Building
traits.description: A Rite usually performed by an entire sept and takes many days and nights, this results in the opening of a new caern.

traits.name: Rite of the Wolf Reborn
traits.description: A Rite used to help a Garou who has Lost the Wolf.

traits.name: Rite of the Whispering Field
traits.description: A Rite used at a caern to allow participants to become attuned to themselves and their surroundings to detect trespassers.

traits.name: Rite of the Shrouded Glen
traits.description: A Rite used to hide a caern from mundane individuals.

traits.name: Rite of the Borne Word
traits.description: Use a friendly spirit to deliver a secure message. The spirit must be capable of traveling swiftly, i.e a bird spirit, a wind spirit, etc.

traits.name: Rite of the Broken Sun (Broken Son)
traits.description: A rite used as a severe form of punishment, usually after the target has undergone several Rite of Shames. A successful use of this rite stops the targets ability to shapeshift and call on mystical rites besides the Rite of Contrition.

traits.name: Rite of Caging
traits.description: This rite makes it more difficult for a creature in the physical world to interact with the Umbra. It can even remove someone from the Umbra. Cannot be used on spirits.

traits.name: Rite of Chiminage
traits.description: This rite allows a Garou to better understand the wants and needs of a spirit.

traits.name: Rite of the Grim Reach
traits.description: This rite turns a large area more hostile and even dangerous. Can be used to protect caerns or places important to Garou.

traits.name: Rite of the Living Tale
traits.description: This rite adds an olfactory dimension to the telling of a story, making it all the more immersive for your listeners. Especially appreciated by Lupus-born.

traits.name: Rite of the Moon's Fickle Grace
traits.description: The performer asks Luna herself for a bit of luck. It can only be performed under a full moon and has the potential to both help and harm you.

traits.name: Rite of Renunciation
traits.description: This rite allows a Garou to gracefully leave their tribe, becoming Forsworn when completed.

traits.name: Rite of the Sacred Scar
traits.description: This rite marks a Garou's flesh with a mystical scar or tattoo related to their renown. This marking allows for an additional point of renown on all gifts and Rites appropriate to the glyph they have been marked with.

traits.name: Rite of Shared Fury
traits.description: A rite that allows the participants to pool their rage and then redistribute it amongst all involved. Requires a performance of violence or confrontation between members.

traits.name: Rite of Passage
traits.description: Before they are called Garou, Kin as set on a dangerous task to prove they have the means and represent what it means to be a true werewolf.

traits.name: Satire Rite
traits.description: A Rite used to ridicule another.

traits.name: Rite of Accomplishment
traits.description: A rite used to honor a Garou for the trials they have endured to reach their current standing.

traits.name: Gathering for the Departed
traits.description: A Rite to honor the newly dead.

traits.name: Rite of the Winter Wolf
traits.description: A Rite used for the too-wounded or too-old Garou to end their lives; if done by two members at the same time, they fight to the death in ritual combat.

traits.name: Rite of the Ban Shared
traits.description: All participants in this rite agree to adhere to a Ban not of their own tribe. There are not any mechanical interactions tied to this, purely role-play.

traits.name: Cleansing Rite
traits.description: A rite that uses sacred materials to cleanse the spirit and physical form of a Garou. Often done after encounters with powerful Banes or Black Spiral Dancers.

traits.name: The Long Vigil
traits.description: A rite in which all associated packs gather together, temporarily putting aside all animosity to celebrate Helios, praise Luna and tell the tales of their victories over the past several years. When all is said and done, all involved burn their trophies and make a final appeal to Luna and Helios to guard Gaia to the last.

traits.name: Pledging Rite
traits.description: A rite that is used by a Pack to define a specific goal such as ;Destroy my local oil plant' - When the goal is completed storyteller's may grant additional glory or mitigate chagrin.

traits.name: The Renewal Circle
traits.description: A rite in which a Garou who has been struggling emotionally or spiritually is joined by their allies and friends in silence, awaiting their moment to support their friend in need.

categories.name: Blood Sorcery Rituals
categories.description: Rituals are Blood Sorcery practices that require extended preparation and precise execution.

traits.name: Astromancy
traits.description:
Learn information such as Skills, Desires and Convictions about someone

If you know the correct Birth or Embrace date, you can add 1 die to the Ritual pool. This does not stack if you know both.

traits.name: Beelzebeatit
traits.description:
Animals avoid the area.

Nothing prevents directed or controlled creatures from entering.

traits.name: Bind the Accusing Tongue
traits.description:
Prevent someone from communicating something negative about the caster

Victim can break free by rolling Composure + Resolve.

traits.name: Blood Apocrypha
traits.description:
Embed messages into Blood or vessels.

The first person receives the message if they are the intended recipient or if they have A Taste For Blood.

traits.name: Blood Walk
traits.description:
Learn characters generation, name, and sire.

Requires one Rouse Check from the subject to be performed.

traits.name: Bloody Message
traits.description:
Make a message appear to a specific type of person then disappear once read.

Make the Ritual Roll when enchanting the surface.

traits.name: Blood to Water
traits.description:
Turn blood into water.

Removes all traces of blood.

traits.name: Clinging of the Insect
traits.description:
Cling to walls like an insect.

The user must cling with both their hands and feet.

traits.name: Coax the Garden
traits.description:
Use plant life to defend.

The plants will target anyone who isn't the caster.

traits.name: Craft Bloodstone
traits.description:
Craft a tracking stone.

A caster may have up to as many stones as they have Resolve.

traits.name: Douse the Fear Errata
traits.description:
Remove their fear of fire temporarily.

The power wears off once the scene ends.

traits.name: Enrich the Blood
traits.description:
Make a mortal more nourishing.

This does not work on Kindred vitae.

traits.name: Herd Ward (Minor)
traits.description:
Ward a single kine of a herd to prevent unauthorized feeding.

Make the Ritual Roll when someone else feeds off the herd.

traits.name: Letter Ward
traits.description:
Place a ward on a letter

Make the Ritual Roll if anyone other than the intended recipient opens it.

traits.name: Revealing the Crimson Trail
traits.description:
Reveal traces of spilled blood.

Very old traces require a Resolve + Awareness test.

traits.name: Seal the Brand
traits.description:
Make a tattoo permanent on a vampire

The process inflicts 1 Superficial damage

traits.name: Shared Memory
traits.description:
Observe another's Memoriam.

Participants observe the events and can offer advice but cannot directly influence the events.

traits.name: Wake with Evening’s Freshness
traits.description:
Awakens the caster at any signs of danger during day sleep.

Do not make the Ritual roll until true danger appears.

traits.name: Ward Against Ghouls
traits.description:
Protect themselves against Ghouls.

Uses standard rules for Wards.

traits.name: Blood Missive
traits.description:
Send messages through blood.

The first person to taste the blood, receives it as if it was intended for them or they possess A Taste for Blood.

traits.name: Sanguine Tidings
traits.description:
Make a message appear on a mirror when a type of person comes nearby.

It disappears from the mirror once it's been read once.

traits.name: As Fog on Water
traits.description:
Walk on water silently.

This can be ended early or kept to allow them to walk on water for the rest of the night.

traits.name: Calling the Aura’s Remnants
traits.description:
Speak with the residual aura of someone who has died.

The aura only has memories up to the time of death.

traits.name: Calix Secretus
traits.description:
Store vitae for a later use.

Two Rouse Checks stored will slake one Hunger.

traits.name: Communicate with Kindred Sire
traits.description:
Creates a long-distance telepathic link between Sire and Childe

Major disturbance on either side breaks the connection.

traits.name: Craftmaster
traits.description:
Temporarily gain dots and specialty in certain skills (Academics, Craft, Performance or Science)

Dots replace any skill that caster already has but gain an extra die if they already have the specialty. On a total failure, caster takes Aggravated Damage.

traits.name: Depths of Nightmare
traits.description:
Curse someone with nightmares that cause Willpower Damage, which can't be healed during the Ritual's duration.

On a total failure, the victim has pleasant dreams and is pointed towards the caster.

traits.name: Elemental Grasp
traits.description:
Command your chosen element to interfere with a target

Target takes Superficial Health Damage and must make an appropriate roll to continue. Caster gains one die to their Ritual pool if the element is already active, like water during a flood.

traits.name: Enhance Dyscrasia
traits.description:
Allow multiple Kindred to feed from one Dyscrasia without destroying it.

Usually, they will use Enrich the Blood first.

traits.name: Eyes of Babel
traits.description:
By consuming the eyes and tongue of another, they gain the ability to read and speak any language known by the victim.

This Ritual may incur Stains.

traits.name: Illuminate Trail of Prey
traits.description:
Allows the caster to follow the trail of a specific person.

The victim's face must be known by the caster.

traits.name: Le Sang de l'Amour
traits.description:
Create a connection between two people, providing they desire each other at the time the Ritual is cast

Both participants can use Resolve + Awareness to get an estimation of where the other is. On a total failure of the initial Ritual, this roll disorients them and their Composure is temporarily reduced by one.

traits.name: Soporific Touch Errata
traits.description:
Convert a dose of vitae to weaken their victim against mundane manipulations or mind-altering Disciplines.

The Ritual Roll is made after the victim makes contact and is contested against with Stamina + Resolve.

traits.name: Silentia Mortis
traits.description:
Replicates Silence of Death (Obfuscate ●), which creates a radius of silence

If intended for someone other than the caster, they will also have to make their own Rouse Check.

traits.name: Shroud of Silence
traits.description:
Prevent sounds from escaping a room.

Requires vitae from a Kindred who possesses Obfuscate

traits.name: Stolen Memory
traits.description:
Access sire's memories.

Can access memories of grandsire or beyond with Difficulty increase of 2 per generation past their sire.

traits.name: Tiamat Glistens
traits.description:
Attune yourself to a place of power, like a Furcus

Caster gains bonuses to Rituals they cast. Only one caster can be attuned to a place of power. If someone else performs the Ritual, the original caster loses their bonus immediately.

traits.name: Truth of Blood
traits.description:
The caster can discern truth from lies.

The target rolls Composure+Occult to resist. This Ritual cannot get past memory wiping Disciplines.

traits.name: Unseen Underground
traits.description:
Become invisible underground.

Immediately ends if they go above ground, or they take hostile actions, otherwise it only lasts for one hour. Errata

traits.name: Viscera Garden
traits.description:
Grow Blood-addicted plants that can consume corpses

Can be eaten by Kindred, they do not slake hunger but they stay down. Mortals who ingest a plant are more susceptible to Disciplines.

traits.name: Ward against Spirits
traits.description:
Protect themselves against spirits.

Uses standard rules for Wards.

traits.name: Warding Circle against Ghouls
traits.description:
Protect themselves against Ghouls.

Uses standard rules for Wards.

traits.name: Web of Hunger
traits.description:
Avoid the pull of the Beckoning. Errata

Without the dagger, this ritual would be 4th level. With the dagger it can be treated as level 2. Errata

traits.name: Bladed Hands
traits.description:
Sharpens the user's hands into a weapon.

Treated as a light piercing Brawl weapon with a +2 modifier.

traits.name: Bloodless Feast
traits.description:
Purify blood, creating a clear blood-like substance.

Those who consume the clear vitae enough are weaker to diablerie.

traits.name: Blood Sigil
traits.description:
Create a tattoo on a Kindred which also contains a message

Can read the message with a Resolve + Occult roll or by Sense the Unseen (Auspex ●). Caster can remove the Blood Sigil by spending Willpower and touching the tattoo.

traits.name: Communal Vigor
traits.description:
A Pack Priest shares their Blood Potency with their pack.

The Priest gains three bonus dice on Dominate and Presence tests against packmates.

traits.name: Dagon’s Call
traits.description:
The caster can rupture the blood vessels of a victim from afar.

The caster can use this up to two additional times, each costing an additional Rouse Check. The opposing roll is Stamina + Resolve.

traits.name: Deflection of Wooden Doom
traits.description:
This ritual protects them from being staked.

Do not roll the Ritual Roll until staked.

traits.name: Elemental Shelter
traits.description:
Meld with your chosen element, similar to Earth Meld (Protean ●●●)

Fire Kolduns must resist terror frenzy before casting this ritual. Koldun's form can be seen with a Wits + Awareness roll or by Sense the Unseen (Auspex ●). If bonded element is removed, such as the fire going out, the Koldun enters torpor.

traits.name: Essence of Air
traits.description:
Allows for flight.

The Camarilla frowns upon this Ritual due to its Masquerade dangers.

traits.name: Eyes of the Past
traits.description:
See what happened at their current location in the past.

It only holds events within the last five years.

traits.name: Fire in the Blood
traits.description:
Create anguish of fire in one's blood to incapacitate

A victim can only be affected by this Ritual once per night. Errata

traits.name: Firewalker
traits.description:
Allow themselves and their comrades to become resistant to fire.

This ritual can be performed on others, but the fingertip removal must all come from the caster.

traits.name: Galvanic Ruination
traits.description:
Short out electronics and wiring nearby.

Generally, it only affects the range of a warehouse or three-story building, but can be extended by adding 1 Difficulty per additional building.

traits.name: Gentle Mind
traits.description:
Protect the mind of a target against Frenzy.

The caster must share blood with another and cannot cast it upon themselves.

traits.name: Grim Chrysalis
traits.description:
Create a cocoon out of hardened vitae that heals damage taken.

The cocoon itself is hard and protects the user from outside damage to some extent.

traits.name: Haunted House
traits.description:
Make a haven appear as if it's haunted.

The effects last for 10 years.

traits.name: Herd Ward (Major)
traits.description:
Ward a herd against unauthorized feeding by warding the door to protect multiple people within.

Make the Ritual Roll when someone else feeds off the herd.

traits.name: Illusion of Peaceful Death
traits.description:
Make a corpse appear as if it'd died a natural death.

The body must have at least half of the body's blood to succeed.

traits.name: Illusion of Perfection
traits.description:
Become a nondescript person to blend into crowds.

Similar to the ability Mask of a Thousand Faces.

traits.name: Nepenthe
traits.description:
Create a draught that can remove Stains, but prolonged use of it can make those Stains permanent

Using Nepenthe two sessions in a row causes one Stain to become permanent. This is cumulative, e.g. four sessions gives two permanent Stains.

traits.name: One with the Blade
traits.description:
Claim ownership of a weapon and protect it from damage.

Only one weapon can hold this ritual at a time.

traits.name: Sanguine Watcher
traits.description:
Create a small rat out of vitae.

The caster can send the rat to go where they instruct to observe or steal, but the instructions must be very explicit.

traits.name: Seeing with the Sky's Eyes
traits.description:
Observe a target from above

Player can ask one question about location and surrounding per success roll. Critical Wins give 3 extra questions and can gain information about Ambitions, Desires, Convictions and Humanity.

traits.name: Seeking Tiamat
traits.description:
Find veins of the Earth

Critical wins discover the closest vein and points towards 2 Furcae on that vein

traits.name: Sleep of Judas
traits.description:
Incapacitate a vampire by drugging them.

Make the Ritual Roll when the target is drugged.

traits.name: Soul of the Hemonculus
traits.description:
Create a Hemonculus, a shriveled, smaller and weaker version of the caster, who must obey your every command

Immune to the sun but cannot be Embraced, made into a ghoul or have a Blood Bond. Vampires slake no Hunger if they drink from it.

traits.name: Stone of the True Form
traits.description:
Dispel illusions and creatures altered via Disciplines and Rituals

Must throw the stone with Dexterity + Athletics. The target resists the Ritual with Resolve + Occult. On a win, illusions are dispelled, shapeshifters are painfully returned to their original form and beings made through Disciplines, like a Vozhd, are separated to their original components.

traits.name: The Unseen Change
traits.description:
Lupines entering the area are forced into Wolf Form.

If they do not succeed on the Ritual Roll against a contested Willpower test, they enter in Lupus Form.

traits.name: Trespass
traits.description:
Allows the caster to easily sneak around a building, such as by flowing through crevices that blood can fit through

Unlike Incorporeal Passage, the caster can be attacked providing they are able to notice the caster.

traits.name: Viral Haruspex
traits.description:
Gather information from everyone in an area suffering from a certain disease

Caster must drink from someone with the disease within 24 hours before casting this Ritual.

traits.name: Ward against Lupines
traits.description:
Protects themselves against Werewolves.

Uses standard rules for Wards.

traits.name: Warding Circle against Spirits
traits.description:
Protect themselves against spirits.

Uses standard rules for Wards.

traits.name: Balm of Bathory
traits.description:
Make a balm from the blood of a young mortal to make the user appear younger

The balm gives the user a temporary Stunning ( ••••) Merit. However, each subsequent use takes double the amount of mortal blood as the last batch.

traits.name: Compel the Inanimate
traits.description:
Give a simple command to an inanimate object which it follows a few minutes later

The caster must remain in the same general area as the object. Sense the Unseen (Auspex ●) can detect the caster with a Wits + Auspex vs. caster's Composure + Blood Sorcery roll.

traits.name: Defense of the Sacred Haven
traits.description:
Protect a haven with mystical darkness from the sun.

The Ritual roll is made once the sun rises.

traits.name: Egregore Consultation
traits.description:
By hosting an illness, the caster is able to enhance Skills by access the experiences of everyone with that illness in the area

If a Skill is very common in an area, like Finance in New York, the caster can get extra die to their roll, at a maximum of two.

traits.name: Eyes of the Nighthawk
traits.description:
Take control of a carnivorous bird and act through them.

The user can use most non-physical Disciplines through the bird.

traits.name: Feast of Ashes
traits.description:
Stop another Kindred from drinking blood for one night.

The ritual roll is made against the victim’s Resolve + Willpower. The victim can eat the ashes to slake hunger but only down to 3.

traits.name: Guided Memory
traits.description:
With the Blood of another vampire, experience their memories guided by them.

This can be used to unlock Discipline powers, Merits and other gifts.

traits.name: Incorporeal Passage
traits.description:
The caster's form becomes ghost-like.

They may only interact through speech and sight.

traits.name: Innocence of the Child’s Heart
traits.description:
Put up a blockade against Scry the Soul to hide diablerie and other vampiric traits.

There's no record of Nicolai sharing this ritual with his fellow Tremere and is only learned by capturing his notes or by ST discretion.

traits.name: Innocence's Veil
traits.description:
Temporarily make traces of Diablerie undetectable.

Cannot be detected by A Taste for Blood or Scry The Soul

traits.name: Invisible Chains of Binding
traits.description:
Confine a victim to a single spot

The victim is unable to move from that spot for one hour per success in the margin unless the chain is destroyed or removed.

traits.name: Land's Sustenance
traits.description:
Feed from a place of power, turning it into a place of suffering where injuries become more severe.

Lasts until end of the Story before needing to be renewed. Once per Session, they can automatically pass a number of Rouse Checks equal to their margin on the Ritual roll.

traits.name: Rending the Sweet Earth
traits.description:
Pull a vampire from the earth who is using Earth Meld.

The ritual automatically awakens the other vampire unless they are in torpor or the Ritual Roll is a Critical Win.

traits.name: Riding the Earth's Vein
traits.description:
Travel from one furcus to another random one.

The caster has no control over where they end up. This Ritual is one way and only activates where it was first cast

traits.name: Protean Curse
traits.description:
Transform a target into a bat similar to Metamorphosis.

This cannot be used on the caster.

traits.name: Ward against Cainites
traits.description:
Protects themselves against Kindred.

Uses standard rules for Wards. A vampire examining this ward may read the name of the caster with an Intelligence + Auspex vs Intelligence + Blood Sorcery roll.

traits.name: Warding Circle against Lupines
traits.description:
Protects themselves against Werewolves.

Uses standard rules for Wards.

traits.name: Seek the Gathered Vitae
traits.description:
Discovered gatherings of Kindred with a collective Blood Potency of 13 or higher.

Ghouls count as 1/4th and Duskborn count as 1/2.

traits.name: Antebrachia Ignium Errata
traits.description:
Set their arms on fire

The user is only resistant to fire on their arms.

traits.name: Atrocity's Release
traits.description:
Reverses the effects of Diablerie

Can be resisted with a Resolve + Blood Sorcery test.

traits.name: Dominion
traits.description:
Stop the use of Animalism, Auspex, Dominate, and Presence within a building.

The area of effect is determined by the amount of Rouse Checks.

traits.name: Eden’s Bounty
traits.description:
Drain blood from nearby creatures

For the remainder of the chapter, mortals suffer 1 die penalty to Physical rolls and take 1 Aggravated health damage.

traits.name: Elemental Attack
traits.description:
The Koldun commands their chosen element to attack a foe. This can be a Chain Ritual

If their element is already active, like Air during a storm, they get an extra die to their Ritual pool. If Chained with Elemental Grasp and Tiamat Glistens, the Ritual becomes a natural disaster, e.g. Tornado, Magma Flow or Tsunami

traits.name: Escape to True Sanctuary
traits.description:
Create a one-way portal.

A caster may only have one set of these circles active at a time.

traits.name: Fisher King
traits.description:
Makes the caster one with their land, giving them access to it's secrets. This can be a Chain Ritual

Can make a Wits + Streetwise or Survival roll to ask questions about the land. The caster can ask one extra question per session. This Ritual lasts until the end of the story. If Chained with Land's Sustenance and Compel the Inanimate, the casters gain complete control over the land and the casters can heal a total of 5 Aggravated damage each night

traits.name: Heart of Stone
traits.description:
Turns their heart to stone preventing staking. The Caster also suffers from emotional detachment, taking penalties to Remorse or active Social-related rolls.

They are unable to use Presence during this ritual's use but gain a bonus when resisting its use on them.

traits.name: Reawakened Vigor
traits.description:
Regain Blood Potency faster after extended torpor.

Inflicts aggravated damage to anyone other than the caster.

traits.name: Shaft of Belated Dissolution
traits.description:
A stake that will seek out another vampire's heart and will cause final death if it finds it.

Even if an attack with this stake fails to hit the heart, if it hits, a splinter will break and move towards the heart to eradicate the victim.

traits.name: Simulacrum Gate
traits.description:
Create a gate to teleport multiple vampires.

There is only one of these known to exist currently.

traits.name: Transferring the Soul
traits.description:
Take over a body from diablerie.

This ritual requires another Kindred who knows Oblivion ●●●●●. It is possible to be done by one Kindred who possess level 5 in both powers.

traits.name: Warding Circle against Cainites
traits.description:
Protects themselves against Kindred.

Uses standard rules for Wards.

categories.name: Oblivion Ceremonies
categories.description: Ceremonies are Oblivion practices that require extended preparation and precise execution.

traits.name: Gift of False Life
traits.description:
Raise either a single corpse or a group to perform simple single or repetitive tasks.

Corpses are mindless creatures and do not defend themselves from attacks, decaying as normal for
 the duration of the ceremony.

traits.name: Knowing Stone
traits.description:
Identify the locations and existences of specific ghosts.

They must know the ghost's name.

traits.name: Summon Spirit
traits.description:
Summon a spirit from the Underworld.

The wraith will disappear at the end of the scene unless a ceremony is used to keep them there
 such as Compel Spirit.

traits.name: Traveler's Call
traits.description:
Summon another Shalimite.

The victim can choose to ignore it, taking -2 to all pools in relation to concentration for one
 scene. This ceremony is connected to the Cult of
 Shalim.

traits.name: Ashen Relic
traits.description:
Preservation of a Kindred's body.

Three successes in the margin preserves more.

traits.name: Awaken the Homuncular Servant
traits.description:
Create spies and stalkers out of body parts or small animals.

The servant can scale or hop walls and hide, but it cannot speak. It is only able to
 telepathically communicate single images to its creator.

traits.name: Blinding the Alloy Eye
traits.description:
Cameras cannot perceive the user clearly.

Do not make the ritual roll until the effect is active. This ceremony is connected to the Sabbat.

traits.name: Compel Spirit
traits.description:
Bend a wraith to a vampire's will.

The ritual roll is made against the wraith's Resolve + Composure or Willpower.

traits.name: Maw of Ahriman
traits.description:
Open a portal to the Abyss, usually
 within their mouth.

The user cannot speak till it is either cancelled or the next sunrise.

traits.name: Create Corpse Suit
traits.description:
Sew a garment made out of mortal flesh that warns the wearer of subtle signs of danger

The garment is semi-sentient and needs to be persuaded with a test. While worn, the wearer gains a
 Folkoric Bane and Folkloric Block of the Storyteller's Choice

traits.name: Create Flesh Golem
traits.description:
Build a flesh golem from parts of a corpse

Can be assembled manually or with the help of Fleshcrafting

traits.name: Fortezza Sindonica
traits.description:
Create a barrier that harms wraiths.

Do not make the Ritual Roll until a wraith attempts to pass. This ceremony is connected to the Hecata.

traits.name: Harrowhaunt
traits.description:
Keep unwanted visitors from their haven.

Vampires are able to enter making a fear Frenzy check each turn while inside. This ceremony is
 connected to the Sabbat.

traits.name: Host Spirit
traits.description:
Allows the user to open their body to a ghost for possession.

Alongside the bonus of +2 to Physical Attribute rolls and +2 health, the wraiths Skills can be
 substituted for the vampire’s own at Storyteller discretion. Errata

traits.name: Knit the Veil
traits.description:
Turn a shroud impenetrable.

When not disturbed this effect does not change for as many nights as the user's Oblivion rating.

traits.name: Name of the Father
traits.description:
Strike a victim's mind with the empty void of Oblivion, leaving them paralyzed.

The victim can spend willpower equal to the number of turns they would remain paralyzed to free
 themselves. This ceremony is connected to the Cult
 of Shalim.

traits.name: Shallow Slumber
traits.description: Reduces time spent in torpor.

traits.name: Shambling Hordes
traits.description:
Raise a group of aggressive walking dead.

If left without commands, they attack anyone around them other than their master.

traits.name: Wisdom of the Dead
traits.description:
Gain information from a corpse skull or head.

Add two dice to their dice pool if they are still carrying the skull/head for the rest of the
 night.

traits.name: Befoul Vessel
traits.description:
Turn a mortal's blood into poison for other vampires.

This power leaves no trace other than the mortal dying the next night with the smell of mildew and
 sweat. This ceremony is connected to the Sabbat.

traits.name: Bind the Spirit
traits.description:
Bind a wraith to a specific location or person.

Bound wraiths have the same powers as specters.

traits.name: Bind to Mortal Form
traits.description:
Extend the life of a mortal without ghouling them.

The ceremony does not keep the mortal young, and continues to age both physically and mentally,
 albeit past their natural lifespan.

traits.name: Death Rattle
traits.description:
Inflict the sensory experience of a wraith's death onto a target.

The victim contests the ritual roll with Composure + Resolve.

traits.name: Split the Veil Errata
traits.description:
Create a tear into a shroud to allow wraiths to enter.

This Ceremony reduces the density of the veil and can reduce it to absent, allowing wraiths to
 come through. The veil increased to frayed after the session ends, stopping any more
 wraiths. Errata

traits.name: Ex Nihilo
traits.description:
Enter the Shadowlands

The Shadowlands have several rules that do not exist in the world of the living.

traits.name: Gift of True Life
traits.description:
Extend a mortal's life by shortening the life of another

The receiving mortal ages half as quickly until the extra lifespan granted has been spent, after
 which, they begin to age normally.

traits.name: Lazarene Blessing
traits.description:
Bring a fresh corpse back to life

This lasts until the body dies again or the wraith is exorcised from the host.

traits.name: Pit of Contemplation
traits.description:
Cast an enemy into Oblivion itself.

Mortals sucked in are instantly killed. This ceremony is connected to the Cult of Shalim.

name: Advantages
description: Benefits a character has over 'normal' folk.

categories.name: Backgrounds
categories.description: Backgrounds describe advantages of relationship, circumstance, and opportunity: material possessions, social networks, and the like.

subcategories.name: Allies
subcategories.description: Individuals who are willing to help a character or another human who has a vendetta against them.

traits.name: Allies
traits.description: A group who will support or aid the Character. Family, friends, or an organization that has loyalty. Build them between (• - •••) Effectiveness and (•-•••) Reliability, the maximum amount of total points is 6. Effectiveness defines how proficient they are at a task. Reliability determines how dependable they are.

subcategories.name: Contacts
subcategories.description: Individuals who can get the character information, items or other things of value.

traits.name: Contacts
traits.description: Individuals who can get the character information, items or other things of value.

subcategories.name: Fame
subcategories.description: The public notoriety of a character, for good or bad.

traits.name: Fame
traits.description: Mortal fame is a dangerous game, the character might have once been a pop singer, actress, or other celebrity. There is a downside as people may remember the character as their face is plastered on a nearby billboard. Fame can be bought to apply in Vampire society as well.

subcategories.name: Influence
subcategories.description: The ability they have to influence how other groups react or act.

traits.name: Influence
traits.description: They have sway in mortal communities, be they political, through financial status and prestige, or manipulation. By default, this merit only applies to a specific group or region of the city.

subcategories.name: Safe House
subcategories.description: Represents the degree of security and distinction beyond a place to sleep and eat. Without dots in this background, the character still has a place to call home but it is not secure or special in any way.

traits.name: Safe House
traits.description: Represents the degree of security and distinction beyond a place to sleep and eat. Without dots in this background, the character still has a place to call home but it is not secure or special in any way.

traits.name: Hidden Armory
traits.description: Each dot adds one pistol and one firearm inside the safe house, safely concealed.

traits.name: Cell
traits.description: Allows two prisoners to be stored inside. Each additional dot either allows the character to store twice as many prisoners or adds +1 to the attempts to escape.

traits.name: Watchmen
traits.description: Each dot supplies 4 Average Mortals and one Gifted Mortal to watch over the safe house.

traits.name: Laboratory
traits.description: Each dot of this merit contributes to dice rolls related to one Science or Technology specialty or for Alchemy pools.

traits.name: Library
traits.description: Each dot of this merit contributes to dice rolls for one Academics, Investigation or Occult specialty. Small safe houses are limited to one dot.

traits.name: Location
traits.description: The place in which this safe house resides gives a +2 dice bonus (or +2 enemies Difficulty) on relevant rolls. Example, living in the heart of downtown allows the character to have a +2 bonus to etiquette rolls to pick up local rumors.

traits.name: Luxury
traits.description: Rich and full of value, the safe house is well decorated with high-end décor and items. +2 dice bonus to Social tests when mortals are inside the safe house. Without at least 3 dots in Resources, these items are stolen or illegally obtained.

traits.name: Postern
traits.description: The safe house has some kind of secret exit that allows them a safe passage out. For each dot of this merit add one die to pools of evasion or escaping surveillance near the safe house.

traits.name: Security System
traits.description: For each dot of this merit, add one die to pools to resist unwelcome guests into the safe house.

traits.name: Warding
traits.description: This safe house possesses some type of magic warding that repels supernatural entities. Each dot of this merit adds one die to pools to resist supernatural scrying and whatever else the Storyteller allows.

traits.name: Holy Ground
traits.description: The safe house has significance to the character's cult, granting them the ability to call upon a large group of cultists to protect their safe house once per story

traits.name: Shrine
traits.description: A shrine is present in the safe house, adding a bonus equivalent to the dots in searching, preparing or otherwise obtaining Ritual or Ceremony ingredients.

traits.name: Business Establishment
traits.description: The safe house is rented out to a business or is run as a business by the Kindred themselves. This gives both benefits and drawbacks, such as a ready stream of income but also being very much on the grid and local enforcement being aware of the location. This reduces the safe house's base dots by one for pools involving the safe house's privacy and defenses against either (pick one) financial or criminal intrusions. Dots in this merit roughly equal Resources but do not stack onto existing Resources for the character

traits.name: Furcus
traits.description: The safe house is located on veins of the earth or a frayed spot in the Veil. Each dot in this merit adds one die to Rituals or Ceremony dice pools used at the furcus

traits.name: Machine Shop
traits.description: Each dot of this merit adds one dice to the pool for Craft rolls. It also adds this to other tests related to building, repairing, or disassembling machinery or equipment.

traits.name: Surgery
traits.description: Each dot of this merit adds one die to the pool for Medicine. It also adds this to other tests related to surgery, healing, or medical treatments.

traits.name: Panic Room
traits.description: The ability to house individuals and breaching this requires a base Difficulty of 5, this can also be applied to those held captive. Each extra dot allows either twice as many individuals with a cap of 32 in large safe houses or adds +1 to the breach/escape Difficulty. This is not available to 1 dot safe houses.

subcategories.name: Herd
subcategories.description: Merits and flaws pertaining to the vampire's ability to have free access to mortal blood through forming a group of willing vessels.

traits.name: Herd
traits.description: A group of vessels that the character may feed from without concern, though they are less loyal than retainers. They can slake Hunger worth the dot value of the Herd each week freely and no roll is required.

subcategories.name: Mask
subcategories.description: The status of the kindred's identification.

traits.name: Mask
traits.description: A fake identity that allows the character to keep their true selves away from mortal's prying eyes, including getting bank accounts, a birth certificate and everything else a vampire might need to masquerade as a human or for a mortal to hide their true identity.

traits.name: Zeroed
traits.description: All of the character's past self has been purged from all systems as if they never existed. The character must have a 2-dot mask in order to take this.

traits.name: Cobbler
traits.description: The ability to create or source out masks. Making a mask takes 3 days per dot. The character must have a 2-dot mask in order to take this.

traits.name: Faked Death
traits.description: As long as you keep a low profile and a new identity nobody from your old life is going to be looking for yo including Enemies, Stalkers, and orgs. You do maintain a limited relationship with any Contacts. The character must have a 2-dot mask in order to take this.

subcategories.name: Mawla
subcategories.description: Relationship with other Vampires

traits.name: Mawla
traits.description: Another kindred who has taken them under their wing to mentor them.

subcategories.name: Resources
subcategories.description: Ready access to money

traits.name: Resources
traits.description: Cash flow, be it from stock trading or inheritance to working as a barista at night.

subcategories.name: Retainers
subcategories.description: Loyal servants or assistants; they can be paid employees, longtime stewards, ghouls, blood bonds, etc.

traits.name: Retainers
traits.description: Loyal followers who will do the character's bidding.

subcategories.name: Status
subcategories.description: Reputation and standing within their community or faction

traits.name: Status
traits.description: The character has built a name for themselves in their Faction.

traits.name: City Secrets
traits.description: This grants knowledge about the city's Kindred power structure. If this secret is about mortal business it's only a way to explain Influence. This information can be sold at a high price, but its value lies in protection as the people involved may not want this information sold off and will do their best to keep you happy, for a time anyway. This can only be taken at a maximum of three times with each being a different secret.

subcategories.name: Mentor
subcategories.description: Relationship with either one or a group who look out for them, offer them guidance, information, or aid in other ways once in awhile. Generally a group costs one more dot than a single mentor of the equivalent level, for example, a cell of three experienced Hunters might be a four dot group

traits.name: Mentor
traits.description: A similar character type or group who has taken the character under their wing.

traits.name: Generous
traits.description: You can call upon your mentor for a valuable favor once per story. This does not run the usual risk of offending your mentor but you lose a dot from this background each time you do

traits.name: Spirit Guide (Arcanum)
traits.description: Your mentor is some kind of ghost or unearthly being that you studied and formed a rapport with. You have the ability to summon them. They are unable to aid with corporeal matters like law enforcement or politics but they do have reliable knowledge on ghosts.

subcategories.name: Spirit Pact
subcategories.description: The Garou has entered into a mutual pact with a lesser spirit. The spirit will act as an ally and provide help with the best of its abilities when summoned.

traits.name: Spirit Pact
traits.description: Loyal spirit who will aid the Garou.

traits.name: Host
traits.description: The spirit has a physical body it hosts. Spirits without the Companion trait still must be summoned.

traits.name: Companion
traits.description: The spirit can accompany the character whenever they go long as nothing prevents it such as spiritual wards. Without a physical host communication is restricted to the Umbra or other means.

categories.name: Merits
categories.description: Merits are special abilities or advantages that are rare or unique in the general population.

subcategories.name: Linguistics
